416	// RealGitOps implements GitOps via os/exec git commands.
417	type RealGitOps struct{}
418	
419	func (r *RealGitOps) HeadRev(dir string) (string, error) {
420		return r.ResolveRev(dir, "HEAD")
421	}
422	
423	func (r *RealGitOps) ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error) {
424		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", rev).Output()
425		if err != nil {
426			return "", fmt.Errorf("git rev-parse %s: %w", rev, err)
427		}
428		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
429	}
430	
431	func (r *RealGitOps) WorktreeAdd(dir, wtPath, rev string) error {
432		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "worktree", "add", "--detach", wtPath, rev).CombinedOutput()
433		if err != nil {
434			return fmt.Errorf("git worktree add: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
435		}
436		return nil
437	}
438	
439	func (r *RealGitOps) WorktreeRemove(dir, wtPath string) error {
440		_ = internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "worktree", "remove", "--force", wtPath).Run()
441		return nil
442	}
443	
444	func (r *RealGitOps) WorktreeList(dir string) ([]string, error) {
445		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "worktree", "list", "--porcelain").Output()
446		if err != nil {
447			return nil, fmt.Errorf("git worktree list: %w", err)
448		}
449		var paths []string
450		for _, line := range strings.Split(string(out), "\n") {
451			if strings.HasPrefix(line, "worktree ") {
452				paths = append(paths, strings.TrimPrefix(line, "worktree "))
453			}
454		}
455		return paths, nil
456	}
457	
458	func (r *RealGitOps) WorktreePrune(dir string) error {
459		return internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "worktree", "prune").Run()
460	}
461	
462	// UpdateRef updates ref in dir to sha via `git update-ref`.
463	func (r *RealGitOps) UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error {
464		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "update-ref", ref, sha).CombinedOutput()
465		if err != nil {
466			return fmt.Errorf("git update-ref %s: %s: %w", ref, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
467		}
468		return nil
469	}
470	
471	// DeleteRef removes ref from dir via `git update-ref -d`.
472	func (r *RealGitOps) DeleteRef(dir, ref string) error {
473		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "update-ref", "-d", ref).CombinedOutput()
474		if err != nil {
475			return fmt.Errorf("git update-ref -d %s: %s: %w", ref, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
476		}
477		return nil
478	}
479	
480	// IsDirty reports whether dir has any uncommitted changes (tracked modifications or untracked files).
481	func (r *RealGitOps) IsDirty(dir string) (bool, error) {
482		out, _ := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "status", "--porcelain", "--", ".", ":(exclude)"+ExecutionCleanupMetadataFileName).Output()
483		return len(bytes.TrimSpace(out)) > 0, nil
484	}
485	
486	func dirtyWorktreePaths(dir string) []string {
487		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "status", "--porcelain", "--untracked-files=all").Output()
488		if err != nil {
489			return nil
490		}
491		var paths []string
492		seen := map[string]bool{}
493		for _, line := range strings.Split(string(out), "\n") {
494			line = strings.TrimRight(line, "\r")
495			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" || len(line) < 4 {
496				continue
497			}
498			path := strings.TrimSpace(line[3:])
499			if idx := strings.Index(path, " -> "); idx >= 0 {
500				path = strings.TrimSpace(path[idx+4:])
501			}
502			if path == "" || seen[path] {
503				continue
504			}
505			seen[path] = true
506			paths = append(paths, path)
507		}
508		return paths
509	}
510	
511	// EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs returns git pathspec-exclusion fragments
512	// applied when landEvidence / VerifyCleanWorktree stage the evidence
513	// directory. Only excludes the multi-thousand-line embedded session log
514	// (the single file that actually explodes past provider context
515	// limits). prompt.md, usage.json, manifest.json, and result.json remain
516	// tracked — they are small and serve the audit-trail contract. The
517	// gitignore written by `ddx init` already treats .ddx/executions/*/
518	// embedded as ignored; this pathspec is defence-in-depth for force-add
519	// paths.
520	//
521	// Regression anchor: ddx-39e27896 — session logs landed as tracked
522	// evidence caused retry prompts to balloon past 2M+ tokens and crash
523	// every provider with n_keep > n_ctx.
524	func EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs() []string {
525		return []string{
526			":(exclude,glob).ddx/executions/*/embedded/**",
527		}
528	}
529	
530	// EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs returns git pathspec-exclusion fragments
531	// applied when review-prompt synthesis runs `git show <rev>` over the
532	// evidence commit. Broader than EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs: excludes
533	// prompt.md and usage.json too, because even though they're tracked for
534	// audit, they're execution-artifact noise from the reviewer's
535	// perspective — the reviewer wants to see the implementation diff, not
536	// the prior attempt's prompt or token counters. This also protects
537	// against old commits (pre-fix) that committed the session log
538	// directly.
539	//
540	// Regression anchor: ddx-39e27896.
541	func EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs() []string {
542		return []string{
543			":(exclude,glob).ddx/executions/*/embedded/**",
544			":(exclude,glob).ddx/executions/*/prompt.md",
545			":(exclude,glob).ddx/executions/*/usage.json",
546		}
547	}
548	
549	// SynthesizeCommit stages real file changes, explicitly excluding harness noise
550	// paths, and creates a commit with msg as the commit message. Returns (true, nil)
551	// when a commit was made, (false, nil) when nothing real remained to commit
552	// after exclusions, and (false, err) on failure.
553	func (r *RealGitOps) SynthesizeCommit(dir, msg string) (bool, error) {
554		// Do NOT list already-gitignored paths (.ddx/agent-logs, .ddx/workers) as
555		// :(exclude) pathspecs. Git treats a path named by :(exclude) as explicitly
556		// referenced, so when the path is also .gitignored git emits "The following
557		// paths are ignored by one of your .gitignore files" AND exits 1 — even
558		// though the pathspec is trying to SKIP it. Paths already in .gitignore are
559		// excluded by default; excludes here are only for paths that would
560		// otherwise be tracked.
561		addArgs := []string{
562			"add", "-A", "--",
563			".",
564		}
565		addArgs = append(addArgs, synthesizeCommitExcludePathspecs(dir)...)
566		if err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, addArgs...).Run(); err != nil {
567			return false, fmt.Errorf("staging changes: %w", err)
568		}
569		statusOut, _ := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "diff", "--cached", "--name-only").Output()
570		if len(bytes.TrimSpace(statusOut)) == 0 {
571			return false, nil
572		}
573		if msg == "" {
574			msg = "chore: execute-bead synthesized result commit"
575		}
576		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "commit", "-m", msg).CombinedOutput()
577		if err != nil {
578			return false, fmt.Errorf("synthesize commit: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
579		}
580		return true, nil
581	}
582	
583	// checkpointPreDispatchDirt captures DDx bookkeeping changes as a commit on
584	// the current branch without using the caller checkout's real index or commit
585	// hooks. The checkpoint is intentionally narrow: if the parent checkout also
586	// contains ordinary implementation files, the attempt fails with an actionable
587	// error so those changes can be committed in the bead's substantive
588	// [ddx-<id>] commit instead of being folded into the checkpoint.
589	const preDispatchCheckpointDirtyRefusalPrefix = "checkpoint refused to absorb implementation changes outside DDx bookkeeping: "
590	const preDispatchDirtyPreserveRefPrefix = "refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/"
591	
592	// PreDispatchDirtyPreservation records the recoverable handle DDx created when
593	// it preserved implementation dirt before a watch-mode redispatch.
594	type PreDispatchDirtyPreservation struct {
595		DirtyPaths     []string
596		PreserveRef    string
597		RecoverCommand string
598	}
599	
600	var preDispatchDirtyPathLister = func(projectRoot string) ([]string, error) {
601		return preDispatchCheckpointDirtyPaths(projectRoot)
602	}
603	
604	func normalizePreDispatchDirtyPaths(paths []string) []string {
605		seen := make(map[string]bool, len(paths))
606		normalized := make([]string, 0, len(paths))
607		for _, path := range paths {
608			path = filepath.ToSlash(strings.TrimSpace(path))
609			if path == "" || seen[path] {
610				continue
611			}
612			seen[path] = true
613			normalized = append(normalized, path)
614		}
615		sort.Strings(normalized)
616		return normalized
617	}
618	
619	func intersectPreDispatchDirtyPaths(current, want []string) []string {
620		if len(current) == 0 || len(want) == 0 {
621			return nil
622		}
623		wantSet := make(map[string]bool, len(want))
624		for _, path := range want {
625			wantSet[path] = true
626		}
627		var overlap []string
628		for _, path := range current {
629			if wantSet[path] {
630				overlap = append(overlap, path)
631			}
632		}
633		sort.Strings(overlap)
634		return overlap
635	}
636	
637	func classifyPreDispatchDirtyPaths(dirtyPaths []string) (allowedPaths, blockedPaths []string) {
638		for _, path := range normalizePreDispatchDirtyPaths(dirtyPaths) {
639			if preDispatchCheckpointAllowedPath(path) {
640				allowedPaths = append(allowedPaths, path)
641				continue
642			}
643			blockedPaths = append(blockedPaths, path)
644		}
645		return allowedPaths, blockedPaths
646	}
647	
648	// stablePreDispatchImplementationDirtyPaths re-reads the project dirt and only
649	// treats implementation paths as actionable when they survive the immediate
650	// recheck. This filters transient rename/delete paths that can briefly appear
651	// in watch mode right after a successful land.
652	func stablePreDispatchImplementationDirtyPaths(projectRoot string, dirtyPaths []string) ([]string, []string, error) {
653		normalized := normalizePreDispatchDirtyPaths(dirtyPaths)
654		if projectRoot == "" || len(normalized) == 0 {
655			return normalized, normalized, nil
656		}
657	
658		currentPaths, err := preDispatchDirtyPathLister(projectRoot)
659		if err != nil {
660			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("rechecking pre-dispatch dirt: %w", err)
661		}
662		return intersectPreDispatchDirtyPaths(currentPaths, normalized), currentPaths, nil
663	}
664	
665	func resolveOptionalGitRef(dir, ref string) (string, bool) {
666		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", "-q", "--verify", ref).Output()
667		if err != nil {
668			return "", false
669		}
670		sha := strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
671		if sha == "" {
672			return "", false
673		}
674		return sha, true
675	}
676	
677	func preDispatchDirtyRecoverCommand(preserveRef string) string {
678		if preserveRef == "" {
679			return ""
680		}
681		return "git stash apply " + preserveRef
682	}
683	
684	func preservePreDispatchDirtyPaths(projectRoot string, dirtyPaths []string) (*PreDispatchDirtyPreservation, error) {
685		if projectRoot == "" {
686			return nil, fmt.Errorf("project root is required for pre-dispatch dirty preservation")
687		}
688		normalized := normalizePreDispatchDirtyPaths(dirtyPaths)
689		if len(normalized) == 0 {
690			return nil, fmt.Errorf("no dirty implementation paths to preserve")
691		}
692	
693		var preserved *PreDispatchDirtyPreservation
694		if err := withTrackerLock(projectRoot, func() error {
695			var err error
696			preserved, err = preservePreDispatchDirtyPathsLocked(projectRoot, normalized)
697			return err
698		}); err != nil {
699			return nil, err
700		}
701		return preserved, nil
702	}
703	
704	func preservePreDispatchDirtyPathsLocked(projectRoot string, dirtyPaths []string) (*PreDispatchDirtyPreservation, error) {
705		if err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "rev-parse", "--is-inside-work-tree").Run(); err != nil {
706			return nil, fmt.Errorf("verifying project worktree for pre-dispatch preservation: %w", err)
707		}
708		if out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "rev-parse", "--verify", "HEAD").CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
709			return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolving HEAD for pre-dispatch preservation: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
710		}
711	
712		stashBefore, _ := resolveOptionalGitRef(projectRoot, "refs/stash")
713		preserveID := GenerateAttemptID()
714		preserveRef := preDispatchDirtyPreserveRefPrefix + preserveID
715		stashMsg := "ddx pre-dispatch preserve " + preserveID
716		stashArgs := []string{"stash", "push", "--include-untracked", "--message", stashMsg, "--"}
717		stashArgs = append(stashArgs, dirtyPaths...)
718		if out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, stashArgs...).CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
719			return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving pre-dispatch dirty implementation paths: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
720		}
721	
722		stashAfter, ok := resolveOptionalGitRef(projectRoot, "refs/stash")
723		if !ok || stashAfter == "" || stashAfter == stashBefore {
724			return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving pre-dispatch dirty implementation paths did not create a recoverable stash entry")
725		}
726		if out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "update-ref", preserveRef, stashAfter).CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
727			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pinning pre-dispatch dirty preservation ref: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
728		}
729		_, _ = internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "stash", "drop", "stash@{0}").CombinedOutput()
730	
731		remainingPaths, err := preDispatchDirtyPathLister(projectRoot)
732		if err != nil {
733			return nil, fmt.Errorf("verifying preserved pre-dispatch dirt: %w", err)
734		}
735		if overlap := intersectPreDispatchDirtyPaths(remainingPaths, dirtyPaths); len(overlap) > 0 {
736			return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving pre-dispatch dirty implementation paths left the project worktree dirty: %s", strings.Join(overlap, ", "))
737		}
738	
739		return &PreDispatchDirtyPreservation{
740			DirtyPaths:     append([]string(nil), dirtyPaths...),
741			PreserveRef:    preserveRef,
742			RecoverCommand: preDispatchDirtyRecoverCommand(preserveRef),
743		}, nil
744	}
745	
746	func checkpointPreDispatchDirt(projectRoot, attemptID string) (bool, error) {
747		if err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "rev-parse", "--is-inside-work-tree").Run(); err != nil {
748			return false, nil
749		}
750		headOut, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "rev-parse", "--verify", "HEAD").Output()
751		if err != nil {
752			return false, nil
753		}
754		head := strings.TrimSpace(string(headOut))
755		if head == "" {
756			return false, nil
757		}
758	
759		skipWorktreePaths, err := checkpointSkipWorktreePaths(projectRoot)
760		if err != nil {
761			return false, err
762		}
763	
764		dirtyPaths, err := preDispatchDirtyPathLister(projectRoot)
765		if err != nil {
766			return false, err
767		}
768		if len(dirtyPaths) == 0 {
769			return false, nil
770		}
771	
772		allowedPaths, blockedPaths := classifyPreDispatchDirtyPaths(dirtyPaths)
773		if len(blockedPaths) > 0 {
774			stableBlockedPaths, currentDirtyPaths, err := stablePreDispatchImplementationDirtyPaths(projectRoot, blockedPaths)
775			if err != nil {
776				return false, err
777			}
778			if len(stableBlockedPaths) > 0 {
779				return false, fmt.Errorf(
780					"%s%s; commit or clean those files before rerunning so the bead's [ddx-<id>] substantive commit stays intentional",
781					preDispatchCheckpointDirtyRefusalPrefix,
782					strings.Join(stableBlockedPaths, ", "),
783				)
784			}
785			allowedPaths, _ = classifyPreDispatchDirtyPaths(currentDirtyPaths)
786		}
787		if len(allowedPaths) == 0 {
788			return false, nil
789		}
790	
791		indexFile, err := config.CreateExecutionScratch(projectRoot, "ddx-pre-dispatch-index-*")
792		if err != nil {
793			return false, fmt.Errorf("creating temp checkpoint index: %w", err)
794		}
795		indexPath := indexFile.Name()
796		_ = indexFile.Close()
797		_ = os.Remove(indexPath)
798		defer func() { _ = os.Remove(indexPath) }()
799	
800		gitWithIndex := func(args ...string) ([]byte, error) {
801			cmd := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, args...)
802			cmd.Env = append(cmd.Env, "GIT_INDEX_FILE="+indexPath)
803			return cmd.CombinedOutput()
804		}
805	
806		if out, err := gitWithIndex("read-tree", "HEAD"); err != nil {
807			return false, fmt.Errorf("initializing checkpoint index: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
808		}
809	
810		addArgs := []string{"add", "-A", "--force", "--"}
811		addArgs = append(addArgs, allowedPaths...)
812		if out, err := gitWithIndex(addArgs...); err != nil {
813			return false, fmt.Errorf("staging checkpoint changes: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
814		}
815	
816		changedOut, err := gitWithIndex("diff", "--cached", "--name-only")
817		if err != nil {
818			return false, fmt.Errorf("checking checkpoint diff: %w", err)
819		}
820		if len(bytes.TrimSpace(changedOut)) == 0 {
821			return false, nil
822		}
823	
824		treeOut, err := gitWithIndex("write-tree")
825		if err != nil {
826			return false, fmt.Errorf("writing checkpoint tree: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(treeOut)), err)
827		}
828		tree := strings.TrimSpace(string(treeOut))
829		msg := "chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead " + attemptID
830		commitOut, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot,
831			"-c", "user.name=ddx-checkpoint",
832			"-c", "user.email=checkpoint@ddx.local",
833			"commit-tree", tree, "-p", head, "-m", msg,
834		).CombinedOutput()
835		if err != nil {
836			return false, fmt.Errorf("creating checkpoint commit: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(commitOut)), err)
837		}
838		commit := strings.TrimSpace(string(commitOut))
839	
840		refOut, _ := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "symbolic-ref", "-q", "HEAD").Output()
841		ref := strings.TrimSpace(string(refOut))
842		if ref == "" {
843			ref = "HEAD"
844		}
845		if out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "update-ref", ref, commit, head).CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
846			return false, fmt.Errorf("advancing checkpoint ref: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
847		}
848		if out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "read-tree", "HEAD").CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
849			return false, fmt.Errorf("syncing checkpoint index: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
850		}
851		if err := restoreCheckpointSkipWorktreePaths(projectRoot, skipWorktreePaths); err != nil {
852			return false, err
853		}
854		return true, nil
855	}
856	
857	func checkpointSkipWorktreePaths(projectRoot string) ([]string, error) {
858		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "ls-files", "-t", "-z").Output()
859		if err != nil {
860			return nil, fmt.Errorf("listing skip-worktree paths: %w", err)
861		}
862		var paths []string
863		for len(out) > 0 {
864			recordEnd := bytes.IndexByte(out, 0)
865			if recordEnd == -1 {
866				recordEnd = len(out)
867			}
868			record := out[:recordEnd]
869			if recordEnd < len(out) {
870				out = out[recordEnd+1:]
871			} else {
872				out = nil
873			}
874			if len(record) < 3 || record[0] != 'S' || record[1] != ' ' {
875				continue
876			}
877			path := filepath.ToSlash(string(record[2:]))
878			if path != "" {
879				paths = append(paths, path)
880			}
881		}
882		return paths, nil
883	}
884	
885	func restoreCheckpointSkipWorktreePaths(projectRoot string, paths []string) error {
886		for start := 0; start < len(paths); start += 100 {
887			end := start + 100
888			if end > len(paths) {
889				end = len(paths)
890			}
891			args := append([]string{"update-index", "--skip-worktree", "--"}, paths[start:end]...)
892			if out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, args...).CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
893				return fmt.Errorf("restoring skip-worktree paths after checkpoint index sync: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
894			}
895		}
896		return nil
897	}
898	
899	// isMaterializedSkillSymlink reports whether a path is a skill directory symlink
900	// that has been materialized into a real directory by an external tool. Skill
901	// symlinks (.crush/skills/*, .claude/skills/*, .agents/skills/*) tracked in git
902	// as symlinks (mode 120000) but present in the working tree as directories are
903	// benign and do not indicate uncommitted implementation changes.
904	func isMaterializedSkillSymlink(projectRoot, path string) bool {
905		// Check if path is in a skill directory root
906		skillDirs := []string{".crush/skills", ".claude/skills", ".agents/skills"}
907		var isSkillPath bool
908		for _, skillDir := range skillDirs {
909			if path == skillDir || strings.HasPrefix(path, skillDir+"/") {
910				isSkillPath = true
911				break
912			}
913		}
914		if !isSkillPath {
915			return false
916		}
917	
918		// Check if it's tracked in git as a symlink
919		mode, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot,
920			"ls-files", "--stage", "--", path).Output()
921		if err != nil || len(mode) == 0 {
922			return false
923		}
924		// ls-files --stage output is: "<mode> <object> <stage>\t<file>"
925		// We need to extract the mode field (first field, 120000 for symlink)
926		parts := strings.Fields(string(mode))
927		if len(parts) < 1 {
928			return false
929		}
930		gitMode := parts[0]
931		if gitMode != "120000" {
932			return false
933		}
934	
935		// Check if the working tree has it as a directory
936		fullPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(path))
937		info, err := os.Stat(fullPath)
938		if err != nil {
939			return false
940		}
941		return info.IsDir()
942	}
943	
944	func preDispatchCheckpointDirtyPaths(projectRoot string) ([]string, error) {
945		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot,
946			"status", "--porcelain=v1", "-z", "--untracked-files=all", "--ignored=matching", "--", ".").Output()
947		if err != nil {
948			return nil, fmt.Errorf("listing checkpoint dirt: %w", err)
949		}
950		if len(out) == 0 {
951			return nil, nil
952		}
953	
954		seen := make(map[string]bool)
955		paths := make([]string, 0, 8)
956		for len(out) > 0 {
957			recordEnd := bytes.IndexByte(out, 0)
958			if recordEnd == -1 {
959				recordEnd = len(out)
960			}
961			record := out[:recordEnd]
962			if recordEnd < len(out) {
963				out = out[recordEnd+1:]
964			} else {
965				out = nil
966			}
967			if len(record) < 3 {
968				continue
969			}
970			status := string(record[:2])
971			path := filepath.ToSlash(string(record[3:]))
972			if status == "!!" && !preDispatchCheckpointAllowedPath(path) {
973				continue
974			}
975			if preDispatchCheckpointIgnoredPath(path) {
976				continue
977			}
978			if isMaterializedSkillSymlink(projectRoot, path) {
979				continue
980			}
981			if path != "" && !seen[path] {
982				seen[path] = true
983				paths = append(paths, path)
984			}
985			if record[0] == 'R' || record[0] == 'C' {
986				recordEnd = bytes.IndexByte(out, 0)
987				if recordEnd == -1 {
988					recordEnd = len(out)
989				}
990				record = out[:recordEnd]
991				if recordEnd < len(out) {
992					out = out[recordEnd+1:]
993				} else {
994					out = nil
995				}
996				path := filepath.ToSlash(string(record))
997				if status == "!!" && !preDispatchCheckpointAllowedPath(path) {
998					continue
999				}
1000				if preDispatchCheckpointIgnoredPath(path) {
1001					continue
1002				}
1003				if isMaterializedSkillSymlink(projectRoot, path) {
1004					continue
1005				}
1006				if path != "" && !seen[path] {
1007					seen[path] = true
1008					paths = append(paths, path)
1009				}
1010			}
1011		}
1012		return paths, nil
1013	}
1014	
1015	func preDispatchCheckpointAllowedPath(path string) bool {
1016		switch {
1017		case path == ".ddx/beads.jsonl":
1018			return true
1019		case path == ".ddx/beads-archive.jsonl":
1020			return true
1021		case strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/executions/"):
1022			return true
1023		case strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/metrics/"):
1024			return true
1025		case strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/runs/"):
1026			return true
1027		case path == ExecutionCleanupMetadataFileName:
1028			return true
1029		default:
1030			return false
1031		}
1032	}
1033	
1034	func preDispatchCheckpointIgnoredPath(path string) bool {
1035		switch {
1036		case path == ".ddx/.git-tracker.lock":
1037			return true
1038		case strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/.git-tracker.lock/"):
1039			return true
1040		case path == ".ddx/beads.lock":
1041			return true
1042		case strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/beads.lock/"):
1043			return true
1044		case strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/attachments/"):
1045			return true
1046		case path == ".ddx/run-state.json":
1047			return true
1048		case path == ".ddx/run-state":
1049			return true
1050		case strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/run-state/"):
1051			return true
1052		case preDispatchCheckpointIgnoredExecutionEmbeddedPath(path):
1053			return true
1054		case strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/beads.jsonl.tmp-"):
1055			return true
1056		case path == ".ddx/workers":
1057			return true
1058		case strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/workers/"):
1059			return true
1060		default:
1061			return false
1062		}
1063	}
1064	
1065	func preDispatchCheckpointIgnoredExecutionEmbeddedPath(path string) bool {
1066		const prefix = ".ddx/executions/"
1067		if !strings.HasPrefix(path, prefix) {
1068			return false
1069		}
1070		rest := strings.TrimPrefix(path, prefix)
1071		attemptID, remainder, ok := strings.Cut(rest, "/")
1072		if !ok || attemptID == "" {
1073			return false
1074		}
1075		return remainder == "embedded" || strings.HasPrefix(remainder, "embedded/")
1076	}
1077	
1078	func synthesizeCommitExcludePathspecs(dir string) []string {
1079		candidates := []struct {
1080			pathspec    string
1081			ignoreProbe string
1082		}{
1083			{
1084				pathspec:    ":(exclude).ddx/executions/*/embedded/**",
1085				ignoreProbe: ".ddx/executions/.ddx-check-ignore/embedded",
1086			},
1087			{
1088				pathspec:    ":(exclude).ddx/executions/*/no_changes_rationale.txt",
1089				ignoreProbe: ".ddx/executions/.ddx-check-ignore/no_changes_rationale.txt",
1090			},
1091			// Exclude DDx-managed evidence bundle files written to the attempt
1092			// worktree by execute_bead.go itself. These are committed separately by
1093			// commitEvidenceBundleInWorktree and must not appear in the agent's code
1094			// commit (which SynthesizeCommit creates). Prior to this worktree-evidence
1095			// design, these files lived in projectRoot and were invisible to
1096			// SynthesizeCommit (which runs in wtPath).
1097			{
1098				pathspec:    ":(exclude).ddx/executions/*/prompt.md",
1099				ignoreProbe: ".ddx/executions/.ddx-check-ignore/prompt.md",
1100			},
1101			{
1102				pathspec:    ":(exclude).ddx/executions/*/manifest.json",
1103				ignoreProbe: ".ddx/executions/.ddx-check-ignore/manifest.json",
1104			},
1105			{
1106				pathspec:    ":(exclude).ddx/executions/*/result.json",
1107				ignoreProbe: ".ddx/executions/.ddx-check-ignore/result.json",
1108			},
1109			{
1110				pathspec:    ":(exclude).ddx/executions/*/usage.json",
1111				ignoreProbe: ".ddx/executions/.ddx-check-ignore/usage.json",
1112			},
1113			{
1114				pathspec:    ":(exclude).claude/skills",
1115				ignoreProbe: ".claude/skills",
1116			},
1117			{
1118				pathspec:    ":(exclude).agents/skills",
1119				ignoreProbe: ".agents/skills",
1120			},
1121			{
1122				pathspec:    ":(exclude)" + ExecutionCleanupMetadataFileName,
1123				ignoreProbe: ExecutionCleanupMetadataFileName,
1124			},
1125			{
1126				// Tracker-lock coordination dir (.ddx/.git-tracker.lock/{pid,
1127				// acquired_at}). Present while withTrackerLock is held — must
1128				// not be staged by a SynthesizeCommit running inside the lock
1129				// (regression: HEAD-race fix folded SynthesizeCommit into the
1130				// locked critical section, exposing this directory to
1131				// `git add -A`).
1132				pathspec:    ":(exclude).ddx/.git-tracker.lock",
1133				ignoreProbe: ".ddx/.git-tracker.lock/pid",
1134			},
1135		}
1136	
1137		pathspecs := make([]string, 0, len(candidates))
1138		for _, c := range candidates {
1139			if isGitIgnored(dir, c.ignoreProbe) {
1140				continue
1141			}
1142			pathspecs = append(pathspecs, c.pathspec)
1143		}
1144		return pathspecs
1145	}
1146	
1147	func isGitIgnored(dir, path string) bool {
1148		err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "check-ignore", "-q", "--", path).Run()
1149		return err == nil
1150	}
1151	
1152	// GenerateAttemptID generates a unique attempt identifier.
1153	func GenerateAttemptID() string {
1154		b := make([]byte, 4)
1155		_, _ = rand.Read(b)
1156		return time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405") + "-" + hex.EncodeToString(b)
1157	}
1158	
1159	// GenerateSessionID generates a short session ID for the agent log.
1160	func GenerateSessionID() string {
1161		b := make([]byte, 4)
1162		_, _ = rand.Read(b)
1163		return "eb-" + hex.EncodeToString(b)
1164	}
1165	
1166	// appendBeadRoutingEvidence records a kind:routing evidence entry on the bead
1167	// after an agent run. It is a best-effort operation — errors are silently
1168	// ignored so a store failure never aborts the main execute-bead flow.
1169	//
1170	// The body separates requested passthrough constraints (harness/provider/model
1171	// from the CLI envelope) and requested power bounds from the actual resolved
1172	// values so analytics can distinguish intent from outcome (AC5 of ddx-20047dd5).
1173	func appendBeadRoutingEvidence(appender BeadEventAppender, beadID, harness, provider, model, routeReason, baseURL string, passthrough config.AgentPassthrough, minPower, maxPower, actualPower int) {
1174		if appender == nil || beadID == "" {
1175			return
1176		}
1177		resolvedProvider := provider
1178		if resolvedProvider == "" {
1179			resolvedProvider = harness
1180		}
1181		type routingBody struct {
1182			ResolvedProvider  string   `json:"resolved_provider"`
1183			ResolvedModel     string   `json:"resolved_model,omitempty"`
1184			RouteReason       string   `json:"route_reason,omitempty"`
1185			FallbackChain     []string `json:"fallback_chain"`
1186			BaseURL           string   `json:"base_url,omitempty"`
1187			RequestedHarness  string   `json:"requested_harness,omitempty"`
1188			RequestedProvider string   `json:"requested_provider,omitempty"`
1189			RequestedModel    string   `json:"requested_model,omitempty"`
1190			RequestedMinPower int      `json:"requested_min_power,omitempty"`
1191			RequestedMaxPower int      `json:"requested_max_power,omitempty"`
1192			ActualPower       int      `json:"actual_power,omitempty"`
1193		}
1194		body := routingBody{
1195			ResolvedProvider:  resolvedProvider,
1196			ResolvedModel:     model,
1197			RouteReason:       routeReason,
1198			FallbackChain:     []string{},
1199			BaseURL:           baseURL,
1200			RequestedHarness:  passthrough.Harness,
1201			RequestedProvider: passthrough.Provider,
1202			RequestedModel:    passthrough.Model,
1203			RequestedMinPower: minPower,
1204			RequestedMaxPower: maxPower,
1205			ActualPower:       actualPower,
1206		}
1207		data, err := json.Marshal(body)
1208		if err != nil {
1209			return
1210		}
1211		summary := fmt.Sprintf("provider=%s", resolvedProvider)
1212		if model != "" {
1213			summary += fmt.Sprintf(" model=%s", model)
1214		}
1215		if routeReason != "" {
1216			summary += fmt.Sprintf(" reason=%s", routeReason)
1217		}
1218		_ = appender.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
1219			Kind:    "routing",
1220			Summary: summary,
1221			Body:    string(data),
1222			Actor:   "ddx",
1223			Source:  "legacy agent execute-bead",
1224		})
1225	}
1226	
1227	// costEventBody is the JSON shape persisted in a kind:cost evidence event.
1228	// `ddx bead metrics aggregate` reads these directly so cost rollup never
1229	// has to join against the session index.
1230	type costEventBody struct {
1231		AttemptID    string  `json:"attempt_id"`
1232		Harness      string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`
1233		Provider     string  `json:"provider,omitempty"`
1234		Model        string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
1235		InputTokens  int     `json:"input_tokens"`
1236		OutputTokens int     `json:"output_tokens"`
1237		TotalTokens  int     `json:"total_tokens"`
1238		CostUSD      float64 `json:"cost_usd"`
1239		DurationMS   int     `json:"duration_ms"`
1240		ExitCode     int     `json:"exit_code"`
1241	}
1242	
1243	// appendBeadCostEvidence records a kind:cost evidence entry on the bead with
1244	// per-attempt token and dollar usage. Best-effort: errors are discarded so a
1245	// store failure never aborts the main execute-bead flow. Emits nothing when
1246	// the appender is nil, the beadID is empty, or every cost field is zero
1247	// (e.g., dry-run, no-changes with no provider call).
1248	func appendBeadCostEvidence(appender BeadEventAppender, beadID, attemptID string, body costEventBody) {
1249		if appender == nil || beadID == "" {
1250			return
1251		}
1252		if body.InputTokens == 0 && body.OutputTokens == 0 && body.TotalTokens == 0 && body.CostUSD == 0 {
1253			return
1254		}
1255		body.AttemptID = attemptID
1256		if body.TotalTokens == 0 {
1257			body.TotalTokens = body.InputTokens + body.OutputTokens
1258		}
1259		data, err := json.Marshal(body)
1260		if err != nil {
1261			return
1262		}
1263		var summary string
1264		if body.CostUSD > 0 {
1265			summary = fmt.Sprintf("tokens=%d cost_usd=%.4f", body.TotalTokens, body.CostUSD)
1266		} else {
1267			summary = fmt.Sprintf("tokens=%d", body.TotalTokens)
1268		}
1269		if body.Model != "" {
1270			summary += fmt.Sprintf(" model=%s", body.Model)
1271		}
1272		_ = appender.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
1273			Kind:    "cost",
1274			Summary: summary,
1275			Body:    string(data),
1276			Actor:   "ddx",
1277			Source:  "legacy agent execute-bead",
1278		})
1279	}
1280	
1281	func startRunStateRefresh(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, state RunState) func() {
1282		interval := RunStateRefreshInterval
1283		if interval <= 0 {
1284			interval = 10 * time.Second
1285		}
1286		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
1287		done := make(chan struct{})
1288		go func() {
1289			defer close(done)
1290			ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
1291			defer ticker.Stop()
1292			for {
1293				select {
1294				case <-ctx.Done():
1295					return
1296				case <-ticker.C:
1297					_ = WriteRunState(projectRoot, mergeLatestCandidateCycleRunState(projectRoot, state))
1298				}
1299			}
1300		}()
1301		return func() {
1302			cancel()
1303			<-done
1304		}
1305	}
1306	
1307	func mergeLatestCandidateCycleRunState(projectRoot string, state RunState) RunState {
1308		states, err := ReadRunStates(projectRoot)
1309		if err != nil {
1310			return state
1311		}
1312		for i := range states {
1313			current := states[i]
1314			if state.AttemptID != "" && current.AttemptID != state.AttemptID {
1315				continue
1316			}
1317			if state.AttemptID == "" && state.WorktreePath != "" && current.WorktreePath != "" &&
1318				filepath.Clean(current.WorktreePath) != filepath.Clean(state.WorktreePath) {
1319				continue
1320			}
1321			state.CandidateCyclePhase = current.CandidateCyclePhase
1322			state.CandidateRef = current.CandidateRef
1323			state.CandidateRev = current.CandidateRev
1324			state.CycleIndex = current.CycleIndex
1325			state.ReviewActive = current.ReviewActive
1326			state.RepairActive = current.RepairActive
1327			return state
1328		}
1329		return state
1330	}
1331	
1332	func buildAttemptDiagnostic(projectRoot, wtPath, beadID, attemptID, headRevErr string, gitOps GitOps) *AttemptDiagnostic {
1333		diag := &AttemptDiagnostic{
1334			BeadID:       beadID,
1335			AttemptID:    attemptID,
1336			WorktreePath: wtPath,
1337			HeadRevError: headRevErr,
1338		}
1339		if _, err := os.Stat(wtPath); err == nil {
1340			diag.WorktreePathExists = true
1341		}
1342		if _, err := ReadExecutionCleanupMetadata(wtPath); err == nil {
1343			diag.CleanupMetadataPresent = true
1344		}
1345		if states, err := ReadRunStates(projectRoot); err == nil {
1346			if state := matchingRunStateForAttempt(states, attemptID, wtPath); state != nil {
1347				diag.RunStatePresent = true
1348				copied := *state
1349				diag.RunState = &copied
1350			}
1351		}
1352		if gitOps != nil {
1353			if paths, err := gitOps.WorktreeList(projectRoot); err == nil {
1354				for _, path := range paths {
1355					if filepath.Clean(path) == filepath.Clean(wtPath) {
1356						diag.GitWorktreeRegistered = true
1357						break
1358					}
1359				}
1360			}
1361		}
1362		return diag
1363	}
1364	
1365	func matchingRunStateForAttempt(states []RunState, attemptID, wtPath string) *RunState {
1366		for i := range states {
1367			if attemptID != "" && states[i].AttemptID == attemptID {
1368				return &states[i]
1369			}
1370			if wtPath != "" && states[i].WorktreePath != "" && filepath.Clean(states[i].WorktreePath) == filepath.Clean(wtPath) {
1371				return &states[i]
1372			}
1373		}
1374		return nil
1375	}
1376	
1377	// appendRateLimitRetryEvent records one rate-limit retry on the bead's
1378	// event stream per TD-031 §4 / §8.4 RateLimitRetryContract. The event body
1379	// carries the retry count and wait duration so an audit can reconstruct the
1380	// pause without consulting routing logs.
1381	func appendRateLimitRetryEvent(appender BeadEventAppender, beadID string, info RateLimitRetryInfo) {
1382		if appender == nil || beadID == "" {
1383			return
1384		}
1385		body := map[string]any{
1386			"attempt":     info.Attempt,
1387			"wait_ms":     info.Wait.Milliseconds(),
1388			"source":      info.Source,
1389			"elapsed_ms":  info.Elapsed.Milliseconds(),
1390			"over_budget": info.OverBudget,
1391		}
1392		if info.Result != nil {
1393			if info.Result.Harness != "" {
1394				body["harness"] = info.Result.Harness
1395			}
1396			if info.Result.Provider != "" {
1397				body["provider"] = info.Result.Provider
1398			}
1399			if info.Result.Model != "" {
1400				body["model"] = info.Result.Model
1401			}
1402		}
1403		data, err := json.Marshal(body)
1404		if err != nil {
1405			return
1406		}
1407		summary := fmt.Sprintf("attempt=%d wait=%s source=%s", info.Attempt, info.Wait.Round(time.Millisecond), info.Source)
1408		if info.OverBudget {
1409			summary = "budget exhausted: " + RateLimitBudgetExhaustedReason
1410		}
1411		_ = appender.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
1412			Kind:    RateLimitRetryEventKind,
1413			Summary: summary,
1414			Body:    string(data),
1415			Actor:   "ddx",
1416			Source:  "legacy agent execute-bead",
1417		})
1418	}
1419	
1420	// ExecuteBeadWithConfig is the thin worker: it creates an isolated attempt workspace,
1421	// constructs the agent prompt from bead context, runs the agent harness,
1422	// synthesizes a commit if the agent left uncommitted changes, then cleans up
1423	// the workspace and returns the result. It classifies outcomes as exactly one
1424	// of:
1425	//
1426	//   - task_succeeded: agent exited 0 and produced one or more commits
1427	//   - task_failed:    agent exited non-zero
1428	//   - task_no_changes: agent exited 0, made no commits, and wrote a rationale
1429	//   - task_no_evidence: agent exited 0 but made no commits and wrote no rationale
1430	//
1431	// Merge, UpdateRef, gate evaluation, preserve-ref management, and orphan
1432	// recovery are the parent's responsibility (see LandBeadResult, RecoverOrphans).
1433	//
1434	// Agent dispatch: tests may set runtime.AgentRunner to inject a fake that
1435	// returns canned Result values; when set, it takes precedence over the normal
1436	// dispatch path. Production execute-bead runs with an explicit harness use the
1437	// local Runner path so the worker subprocess receives the execute-bead Git
1438	// isolation environment (PATH wrapper + scrubbed Git-local env). Unpinned
1439	// routes still fall back to the service path.
1440	func ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, beadID string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadRuntime, gitOps GitOps) (*ExecuteBeadResult, error) {
1441		if ctx == nil {
1442			ctx = context.Background()
1443		}
1444		_ = rcfg.Harness()
1445		if runtime.WorkerID == "" {
1446			runtime.WorkerID = os.Getenv("DDX_WORKER_ID")
1447		}
1448		attemptBackend := runtime.AttemptBackend
1449		if attemptBackend == nil {
1450			var backendErr error
1451			attemptBackend, backendErr = ResolveAttemptBackend(rcfg)
1452			if backendErr != nil {
1453				return nil, backendErr
1454			}
1455		}
1456	
1457		resourceChecker := runtime.ResourceChecker
1458		if resourceChecker == nil {
1459			resourceChecker = NewExecutionResourceChecker(projectRoot, gitOps)
1460		}
1461		if _, err := resourceChecker.Check(ctx); err != nil {
1462			var resourceErr *ResourceExhaustedError
1463			if errors.As(err, &resourceErr) {
1464				res := &ExecuteBeadResult{
1465					BeadID:            beadID,
1466					WorkerID:          runtime.WorkerID,
1467					ExitCode:          1,
1468					Error:             resourceErr.Error(),
1469					Reason:            resourceErr.Error(),
1470					Outcome:           ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed,
1471					Status:            ExecuteBeadStatusResourceExhausted,
1472					ProjectRoot:       projectRoot,
1473					ResourceExhausted: &resourceErr.Result,
1474				}
1475				res.FailureMode = ClassifyFailureMode(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Error)
1476				return res, nil
1477			}
1478			return nil, err
1479		}
1480	
1481		attemptID := GenerateAttemptID()
1482	
1483		// Serialize the parent-repo mutating pre-dispatch sequence (tracker
1484		// commit, caller-dirt checkpoint, base resolution, workspace creation)
1485		// under the tracker lock. Without this, concurrent workers race on the
1486		// parent's HEAD ref between unlocked CommitTracker and SynthesizeCommit
1487		// and surface as "cannot lock ref 'HEAD': is at X but expected Y" CAS
1488		// failures (regression: concurrent `ddx work` against the same project).
1489		// The lock is held only across the brief pre-dispatch sequence (~1-2s);
1490		// agent execution that follows is fully parallel.
1491		var baseRev string
1492		var workspace *AttemptWorkspace
1493		if err := withTrackerLock(projectRoot, func() error {
1494			// Commit beads.jsonl before spawning the attempt workspace so its snapshot
1495			// includes any bead metadata updates (e.g. spec-id).
1496			if err := commitTrackerLocked(projectRoot); err != nil {
1497				return err
1498			}
1499			// Checkpoint any remaining caller dirt as a real commit on the current
1500			// branch (FEAT-012 §22, US-126 AC#1). Use a temp-index commit-tree path
1501			// here so parent checkout hooks and runtime artifacts cannot fail the
1502			// dispatch before the isolated worker worktree exists.
1503			if _, err := checkpointPreDispatchDirt(projectRoot, attemptID); err != nil {
1504				return fmt.Errorf("pre-execute-bead checkpoint: %w", err)
1505			}
1506			// Resolve base revision after the tracker + checkpoint commits.
1507			rev, err := resolveBase(gitOps, projectRoot, runtime.FromRev)
1508			if err != nil {
1509				return err
1510			}
1511			baseRev = rev
1512			ws, err := attemptBackend.Prepare(ctx, AttemptBackendPrepareRequest{
1513				ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
1514				BeadID:      beadID,
1515				AttemptID:   attemptID,
1516				BaseRev:     baseRev,
1517				GitOps:      gitOps,
1518			})
1519			if err != nil {
1520				return err
1521			}
1522			workspace = ws
1523			return nil
1524		}); err != nil {
1525			// A disk/resource-exhaustion failure during the pre-dispatch sequence
1526			// (most commonly `git worktree add` running out of space while checking
1527			// out the isolated worktree) must surface as a resource_exhausted
1528			// outcome, not a raw error. The execute-loop releases the claim for a
1529			// resource_exhausted report (parity with the pre-execution resource
1530			// check above); a raw error leaves the bead claimed-but-open and
1531			// execution-ineligible until a manual --unclaim (ddx-f677a50b).
1532			if classifyReadinessSystemReason(err.Error(), nil) == ReadinessSystemReasonResourceExhausted {
1533				res := &ExecuteBeadResult{
1534					BeadID:      beadID,
1535					WorkerID:    runtime.WorkerID,
1536					ExitCode:    1,
1537					Error:       err.Error(),
1538					Reason:      err.Error(),
1539					Outcome:     ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed,
1540					Status:      ExecuteBeadStatusResourceExhausted,
1541					ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
1542				}
1543				res.FailureMode = ClassifyFailureMode(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Error)
1544				return res, nil
1545			}
1546			return nil, err
1547		}
1548		if workspace == nil || strings.TrimSpace(workspace.WorkDir) == "" {
1549			return nil, fmt.Errorf("attempt backend %s did not return a workspace", attemptBackend.Name())
1550		}
1551		wtPath := workspace.WorkDir
1552		if err := excludeCleanupMetadataFromWorktreeGit(wtPath); err != nil {
1553			_ = attemptBackend.Cleanup(ctx, workspace)
1554			return nil, fmt.Errorf("excluding execute-bead cleanup metadata: %w", err)
1555		}
1556		if err := WriteExecutionCleanupMetadata(wtPath, ExecutionCleanupMetadata{
1557			ProjectRoot:  projectRoot,
1558			BeadID:       beadID,
1559			AttemptID:    attemptID,
1560			WorktreePath: wtPath,
1561			Registered:   true,
1562			CreatedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
1563		}); err != nil {
1564			_ = attemptBackend.Cleanup(ctx, workspace)
1565			return nil, fmt.Errorf("writing execute-bead cleanup metadata: %w", err)
1566		}
1567		defer func() {
1568			_ = attemptBackend.Cleanup(ctx, workspace)
1569		}()
1570	
1571		// evidenceCommittedInWt is true when the evidence bundle was successfully
1572		// committed inside the attempt worktree (normal success path). The deferred
1573		// publish below is a no-op in that case; for all other paths it copies the
1574		// bundle from wtPath to projectRoot so VerifyCleanWorktree can commit it.
1575		evidenceDir := filepath.ToSlash(filepath.Join(ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID))
1576		var evidenceCommittedInWt bool
1577		defer func() {
1578			// Runs BEFORE WorktreeRemove (registered later = first in LIFO order).
1579			if evidenceCommittedInWt {
1580				publishEvidenceBundleToProjectRoot(projectRoot, wtPath, filepath.ToSlash(filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "embedded")))
1581			} else {
1582				publishEvidenceBundleToProjectRoot(projectRoot, wtPath, evidenceDir)
1583			}
1584		}()
1585		publishAttemptResult := func(res *ExecuteBeadResult) error {
1586			if res == nil || strings.TrimSpace(res.ResultRev) == "" || res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
1587				return nil
1588			}
1589			return attemptBackend.PublishResult(ctx, workspace, res)
1590		}
1591	
1592		// Publish the live run-state so operators and HELIX can observe what is
1593		// executing without polling the bead tracker (CONTRACT-001 §5). The file
1594		// is removed on completion; a crashed worker leaves a stale file that
1595		// RecoverOrphans sweeps before the next attempt.
1596		runState := RunState{
1597			BeadID:       beadID,
1598			AttemptID:    attemptID,
1599			Harness:      rcfg.Harness(),
1600			Model:        rcfg.Model(),
1601			StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
1602			WorktreePath: wtPath,
1603		}
1604		_ = WriteRunState(projectRoot, runState)
1605		defer func() {
1606			_ = ClearRunStateAttempt(projectRoot, attemptID)
1607		}()
1608	
1609		// Repair project-local skill symlinks whose targets do not resolve inside
1610		// the freshly created worktree.
1611		_ = materializeWorktreeSkills(wtPath)
1612	
1613		// Prepare artifacts (context load, prompt generation).
1614		artifacts, err := prepareArtifacts(projectRoot, wtPath, beadID, attemptID, baseRev, rcfg, runtime)
1615		if err != nil {

1616			res := &ExecuteBeadResult{
1617				BeadID:      beadID,
1618				AttemptID:   attemptID,
1619				WorkerID:    runtime.WorkerID,
1620				BaseRev:     baseRev,
1621				ResultRev:   baseRev, // no commits; ResultRev == BaseRev signals no output
1622				ExitCode:    1,
1623				Error:       err.Error(),
1624				Outcome:     ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed,
1625				ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
1626			}
1627			// Bundle lives in the attempt worktree after the fix; check there.
1628			wtBundleDir := filepath.Join(wtPath, ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID)
1629			if abInfo, _ := os.Stat(wtBundleDir); abInfo != nil && abInfo.IsDir() {
1630				res.ExecutionDir = filepath.Join(ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID)
1631			}
1632			res.FailureMode = ClassifyFailureMode(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Error)
1633			populateWorkerStatus(res)
1634			_ = writeArtifactJSON(filepath.Join(wtBundleDir, "result.json"), res)
1635			// The deferred publish will copy from wtPath to projectRoot before cleanup.
1636			return res, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead context load: %w", err)
1637		}
1638	
1639		// Pre-create the execution bundle dir in the worktree so the agent can write
1640		// artifacts (e.g. no_changes_rationale.txt) without needing to create the
1641		// directory itself. Failures are non-fatal: the agent can create it on demand.
1642		_ = os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(wtPath, artifacts.DirRel), 0o755)
1643	
1644		// Redirect per-run session/telemetry output into the DDx-owned execution
1645		// bundle so the embedded harness does not accumulate state at the worktree root.
1646		embeddedStateDir := filepath.Join(artifacts.DirAbs, "embedded")
1647		if err := os.MkdirAll(embeddedStateDir, 0o755); err != nil {
1648			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating embedded state dir: %w", err)
1649		}
1650		gitIsolationEnv := map[string]string{}
1651		if _, statErr := os.Stat(filepath.Join(wtPath, ".git")); statErr == nil {
1652			var err error
1653			gitIsolationEnv, err = prepareExecuteBeadGitIsolation(projectRoot, wtPath, embeddedStateDir)
1654			if err != nil {
1655				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preparing execute-bead git isolation: %w", err)
1656			}
1657		} else if !os.IsNotExist(statErr) || runtime.AgentRunner == nil {
1658			return nil, fmt.Errorf("preparing execute-bead git isolation: stat worktree .git: %w", statErr)
1659		}
1660	
1661		sessionID := GenerateSessionID()
1662		startedAt := time.Now().UTC()
1663		runState.SessionID = sessionID
1664		_ = WriteRunState(projectRoot, runState)
1665	
1666		runRuntime := AgentRunRuntime{
1667			PromptFile: artifacts.PromptAbs,
1668			Output:     runtime.Output,
1669			WorkDir:    wtPath,
1670			Correlation: map[string]string{
1671				"bead_id":     beadID,
1672				"base_rev":    baseRev,
1673				"attempt_id":  attemptID,
1674				"session_id":  sessionID,
1675				"worker_id":   runtime.WorkerID,
1676				"bundle_path": artifacts.DirRel,
1677				"prompt_file": artifacts.PromptRel,
1678				"prompt_sha":  artifacts.PromptSHA,
1679			},
1680			SessionLogDirOverride: embeddedStateDir,
1681			PermissionsOverride:   "unrestricted", // isolated worktree; writes must not require approval
1682			Role:                  "implementer",
1683			CorrelationID:         beadID + ":" + attemptID,
1684			Env:                   gitIsolationEnv,
1685		}
1686		runRuntime.Env[DDXModeEnvKey] = DDXModeBeadExecution
1687	
1688		if runtime.AgentRunner == nil && runtime.Service == nil {
1689			if harness := strings.TrimSpace(rcfg.Harness()); harness != "" {
1690				runner := NewRunner(Config{SessionLogDir: ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, "")})
1691				runner.WorkDir = projectRoot
1692				runtime.AgentRunner = runner
1693			}
1694		}
1695	
1696		// Operator-cancel mid-attempt poll (ADR-022 §Cancel SLA). The poll
1697		// re-reads the bead's Extra every CancelPollInterval; on
1698		// cancel-requested:true it cancels the dispatch context (which kills the
1699		// agent subprocess at the next syscall boundary) and writes
1700		// cancel-honored:true so subsequent cancel POSTs are silent no-ops.
1701		dispatchCtx, dispatchCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
1702		defer dispatchCancel()
1703		cancelHonored := startCancelPoll(dispatchCtx, dispatchCancel, beadID, runtime.BeadCancel)
1704	
1705		stopRunStateRefresh := startRunStateRefresh(dispatchCtx, projectRoot, runState)
1706		agentResult, agentErr := attemptBackend.Run(dispatchCtx, AttemptBackendRunRequest{
1707			ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
1708			Workspace:   workspace,
1709			Service:     runtime.Service,
1710			AgentRunner: runtime.AgentRunner,
1711			Config:      rcfg,
1712			Runtime:     runRuntime,
1713		})
1714		stopRunStateRefresh()
1715		finishedAt := time.Now().UTC()
1716	
1717		exitCode := 0
1718		tokens := 0
1719		inputTokens := 0
1720		cachedTokens := 0
1721		outputTokens := 0
1722		costUSD := 0.0
1723		resultModel := rcfg.Model()
1724		resultHarness := rcfg.Harness()
1725		resultProvider := ""
1726		actualPower := 0
1727		predictedPower := 0
1728		predictedSpeedTPS := 0.0
1729		predictedCostUSDPer1kTokens := 0.0
1730		predictedCostSource := ""
1731		agentErrMsg := ""
1732		agentStderr := ""
1733		if agentResult != nil {
1734			exitCode = agentResult.ExitCode
1735			tokens = agentResult.Tokens
1736			inputTokens = agentResult.InputTokens
1737			cachedTokens = agentResult.CachedTokens
1738			outputTokens = agentResult.OutputTokens
1739			costUSD = agentResult.CostUSD
1740			if agentResult.Error != "" {
1741				agentErrMsg = agentResult.Error
1742			}
1743			if agentResult.Stderr != "" {
1744				agentStderr = agentResult.Stderr
1745			}
1746			if agentResult.Model != "" {
1747				resultModel = agentResult.Model
1748			}
1749			if agentResult.Provider != "" {
1750				resultProvider = agentResult.Provider

2143	// populateWorkerStatus fills in the Status and Detail fields on a worker result
2144	// based on the task-level Outcome.
2145	func populateWorkerStatus(res *ExecuteBeadResult) {
2146		switch res.Outcome {
2147		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
2148			res.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
2149		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
2150			res.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
2151		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
2152			res.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced
2153		default:
2154			res.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
2155		}
2156		res.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(res.Status, "", res.Error)
2157	}

2159	// commitTrackerLocked commits beads.jsonl if it has uncommitted changes.
2160	// Callers must hold withTrackerLock(projectRoot) before calling. Used by Run()
2161	// to fold tracker commit, checkpoint synthesis, and worktree creation into a
2162	// single critical section so concurrent workers do not race on the parent's
2163	// HEAD ref.
2164	func commitTrackerLocked(projectRoot string) error {
2165		trackerFile := ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot, "beads.jsonl")
2166		info, err := os.Stat(trackerFile)
2167		if err != nil {
2168			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
2169				return nil
2170			}
2171			return fmt.Errorf("checking tracker file: %w", err)
2172		}
2173		if info.IsDir() {
2174			return nil
2175		}
2176	
2177		gitDir, trackerPathspecs := ddxStateGitScope(projectRoot, ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
2178		trackerPathspec := trackerPathspecs[0]
2179	
2180		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), gitDir, "rev-parse", "--is-inside-work-tree").Output()
2181		if err != nil || strings.TrimSpace(string(out)) != "true" {
2182			return nil
2183		}
2184	
2185		msg := fmt.Sprintf("chore: update tracker (execute-bead %s)", time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405"))
2186	
2187		// Re-check inside the lock: a sibling worker may have already
2188		// committed the tracker changes between our pre-lock check and now.
2189		diff, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), gitDir, "diff", "--", trackerPathspec).Output()
2190		if err != nil {
2191			return fmt.Errorf("checking tracker diff: %w", err)
2192		}
2193		if strings.TrimSpace(string(diff)) == "" {
2194			untracked, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), gitDir, "ls-files", "--others", "--exclude-standard", trackerPathspec).Output()
2195			if err != nil {
2196				return fmt.Errorf("checking tracker untracked: %w", err)
2197			}
2198			if strings.TrimSpace(string(untracked)) == "" {
2199				return nil
2200			}
2201		}
2202	
2203		commitOut, err := runGitWithIndexLockRecovery(context.Background(), gitDir, "add", trackerPathspec)
2204		if err != nil {
2205			return fmt.Errorf("staging tracker: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(commitOut)), err)
2206		}
2207		// `git commit` would fail with "nothing to commit" if the file's
2208		// content is byte-identical to HEAD even though `git diff` saw a
2209		// diff (e.g. mode/whitespace race). Bail cleanly in that case.
2210		if cached, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), gitDir, "diff", "--cached", "--", trackerPathspec).Output(); err == nil && strings.TrimSpace(string(cached)) == "" {
2211			return nil
2212		}
2213		commitArgs := ddxStateCommitArgs(projectRoot, gitDir, "commit", "--no-verify", "--only", "-m", msg, "--", trackerPathspec)
2214		commitOut, err = runGitWithIndexLockRecovery(context.Background(), gitDir, commitArgs...)
2215		if err != nil {
2216			return fmt.Errorf("committing tracker: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(commitOut)), err)
2217		}
2218		return nil
2219	}

2221	func resolveBase(gitOps GitOps, workDir, fromRev string) (string, error) {
2222		if fromRev == "" || fromRev == "HEAD" {
2223			rev, err := gitOps.HeadRev(workDir)
2224			if err != nil {
2225				return "", fmt.Errorf("resolving HEAD: %w", err)
2226			}
2227			return rev, nil
2228		}
2229		rev, err := gitOps.ResolveRev(workDir, fromRev)
2230		if err != nil {
2231			return "", fmt.Errorf("resolving --from %q: %w", fromRev, err)
2232		}
2233		return rev, nil
2234	}

2255			AttemptID: attemptID,

2257			BeadID:    beadID,

2260			Requested: executeBeadRequested{

2263				Provider: rcfg.Passthrough().Provider,

2268			},

2283				Result:   artifacts.ResultRel,

2297	func loadBeadContext(wtPath, beadID string) (*bead.Bead, []executeBeadGoverningRef, error) {
2298		store := bead.NewStore(ddxroot.JoinProject(wtPath))
2299		b, err := store.Get(context.Background(), beadID)
2300		if err != nil {
2301			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("loading bead %s from worktree snapshot: %w", beadID, err)
2302		}
2303		return b, ResolveGoverningRefs(wtPath, b), nil
2304	}
2305	
2306	func ResolveGoverningRefs(root string, b *bead.Bead) []executeBeadGoverningRef {
2307		specIDRaw, _ := b.Extra["spec-id"].(string)
2308		specIDRaw = strings.TrimSpace(specIDRaw)
2309		if specIDRaw == "" {
2310			return nil
2311		}
2312	
2313		// spec-id may be a comma-separated list of IDs or paths.
2314		ids := strings.Split(specIDRaw, ",")
2315		graph, _ := docgraph.BuildGraphWithConfig(root)
2316	
2317		var refs []executeBeadGoverningRef
2318		for _, specID := range ids {
2319			specID = strings.TrimSpace(specID)
2320			if specID == "" {
2321				continue
2322			}
2323			if graph != nil {
2324				if doc, ok := graph.Documents[specID]; ok && doc != nil {
2325					refs = append(refs, executeBeadGoverningRef{
2326						ID:    doc.ID,
2327						Path:  filepath.ToSlash(strings.TrimPrefix(strings.TrimPrefix(doc.Path, root), string(filepath.Separator))),
2328						Title: doc.Title,
2329					})
2330					continue
2331				}
2332			}
2333			candidate := filepath.Clean(filepath.Join(root, filepath.FromSlash(specID)))
2334			relCandidate, relErr := filepath.Rel(root, candidate)
2335			if relErr != nil || strings.HasPrefix(relCandidate, ".."+string(filepath.Separator)) || relCandidate == ".." {
2336				continue
2337			}
2338			info, statErr := os.Stat(candidate)
2339			if statErr != nil || info.IsDir() {
2340				continue
2341			}
2342			refs = append(refs, executeBeadGoverningRef{
2343				ID:   specID,
2344				Path: filepath.ToSlash(relCandidate),
2345			})
2346		}
2347		return refs
2348	}

2350	func createArtifactBundle(rootDir, wtPath, attemptID string) (*executeBeadArtifacts, error) {
2351		dirRel := filepath.ToSlash(filepath.Join(ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID))
2352		dirAbs := filepath.Join(wtPath, ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID)
2353		if err := os.MkdirAll(dirAbs, 0o755); err != nil {
2354			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating execute-bead artifact bundle: %w", err)
2355		}
2356		return &executeBeadArtifacts{
2357			DirAbs:      dirAbs,
2358			DirRel:      dirRel,
2359			PromptAbs:   filepath.Join(dirAbs, "prompt.md"),
2360			PromptRel:   filepath.ToSlash(filepath.Join(dirRel, "prompt.md")),
2361			ManifestAbs: filepath.Join(dirAbs, "manifest.json"),
2362			ManifestRel: filepath.ToSlash(filepath.Join(dirRel, "manifest.json")),
2363			ResultAbs:   filepath.Join(dirAbs, "result.json"),
2364			ResultRel:   filepath.ToSlash(filepath.Join(dirRel, "result.json")),
2365			ChecksAbs:   filepath.Join(dirAbs, "checks.json"),
2366			ChecksRel:   filepath.ToSlash(filepath.Join(dirRel, "checks.json")),
2367			UsageAbs:    filepath.Join(dirAbs, "usage.json"),
2368			UsageRel:    filepath.ToSlash(filepath.Join(dirRel, "usage.json")),
2369		}, nil
2370	}

2372	// Load-bearing guardrails (FEAT-022 static-prompt minimum-prompt rule).
2373	// Every static execute-bead prompt string below MUST preserve each item.
2374	// TestExecuteBeadInstructionsLoadBearingGuardrails and
2375	// TestPromptGuardrails_AllPresent enforce this list; add a guardrail here
2376	// AND to both tests when you introduce one.
2377	//
2378	// 24 guardrails (FEAT-022 cross-reference):
2379	//  1. AC checkbox: every AC satisfied by a specific code/test/file (anti-handwave)
2380	//  2. Read named files / referenced specs first, before editing
2381	//  3. Missing-governing fallback note (non-minimal renders only — see
2382	//     executeBeadMissingGoverningText and contextBudget="minimal" branch)
2383	//  4. Commit subject ends with [<bead-id>]
2384	//  5. Commit exactly once, when green
2385	//  6. git add <specific-paths> (prefer explicit staging over globs)
2386	//  7. Never git add -A (worktree may have unrelated WIP)
2387	//  8. Run git/index mutations sequentially; never parallelize git add/commit
2388	//  9. Do not commit red code
2389	// 10. Rerun lefthook run pre-commit after staging when hooks depend on staged files;
2390	//     pre-staging no-staged-files runs do not count as acceptance evidence
2391	// 11. Do not modify files outside bead scope
2392	// 12. Never run `ddx init`
2393	// 13. Keep .ddx/executions/ intact
2394	// 14. Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md unless asked
2395	// 15. Bead description overrides CLAUDE.md / YAGNI defaults
2396	// 16. Reports go under the bead metadata bundle path, never /tmp, committed
2397	//     alongside code
2398	// 17. Write no_changes_rationale.txt before exiting empty
2399	// 18. Step 0 size-check + decomposition (ddx bead create / dep add / update)
2400	// 19. Current-bead lifecycle mutations stay orchestrator-owned; only Step 0
2401	//     parent-note updates may touch current-bead tracker state
2402	// 20. Address every BLOCKING <review-findings> item; no no_changes with blocking findings open
2403	// 21. Stop after the commit (Agent post-commit runaway guard)
2404	// 22. Agent variant only: use tool calls, not `bash: cat`/`rg`/`ls`
2405	// 23. Long-running matrix/benchmark beads (FEAT-010): require explicit matrix
2406	//     plan before running expensive commands; document output path and completion criterion
2407	// 24. Prohibit rerunning identical long-running commands without documenting why
2408	//     prior output is invalid and what changed before the retry
2409	
2410	// instrStep0SizeCheck is the shared Step 0 size-check + decomposition recipe.
2411	// Both variants emit it verbatim; per-variant preamble runs before it.
2412	const instrStep0SizeCheck = `
2413	
2414	## Step 0: size check
2415	
2416	Too big if any holds:
2417	
2418	- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
2419	- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
2420	- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
2421	- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
2422	- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
2423	- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.
2424	
2425	If too big, decompose:
2426	
2427	1. ` + "`ddx bead create`" + ` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2428	2. ` + "`ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>`" + ` to record edges.
2429	3. ` + "`ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`" + `.
2430	4. Write ` + "`no_changes_rationale.txt`" + ` under the bead metadata ` + "`bundle`" + ` path with child IDs, then stop.
2431	
2432	Decomposition alone is success. Do not mix it with implementation.`
2433	
2434	// instrNoChangesContract is the shared NoChangesContract (TD-031 §8.1) rule.
2435	const instrNoChangesContract = `
2436	
2437	## no_changes contract
2438	
2439	The ` + "`no_changes_rationale.txt`" + ` file under the bead metadata ` + "`bundle`" + ` path must contain one of:
2440	
2441	- ` + "`verification_command: <cmd>`" + ` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
2442	- ` + "`status: open`" + ` + ` + "`reason: <retryable>`" + ` — open, smart retry.
2443	- ` + "`status: proposed`" + ` + ` + "`reason: <operator needed>`" + ` — operator lane.
2444	- ` + "`status: blocked`" + ` + ` + "`reason: <external blocker>`" + ` — blocked lane.
2445	
2446	No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and ` + "`status: needs_investigation`" + ` are rejected.
2447	
2448	For orchestrator decomposition, add ` + "`orchestrator_action: decompose`" + ` alongside ` + "`status: open`" + `.`
2449	
2450	// instrInvestigationReports is the shared report-output rule.
2451	const instrInvestigationReports = `
2452	
2453	## Reports
2454	
2455	Reports go under the bead metadata ` + "`bundle`" + ` path in ` + "`.ddx/executions/`" + `. **Never write reports to ` + "`/tmp`" + ` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write ` + "`<short-name>.md`" + ` there. Commit it with the code.`
2456	
2457	// instrReviewGate is the shared review-as-gate rule.
2458	const instrReviewGate = `
2459	
2460	## Review gate
2461	
2462	- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
2463	- Address every BLOCKING ` + "`<review-findings>`" + ` item; do not declare ` + "`no_changes`" + ` with blocking findings open.`
2464	
2465	// instrBeadOverride is the shared mode + bead-overrides-defaults rule.
2466	const instrBeadOverride = `
2467	
2468	## Mode and overrides
2469	
2470	DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.`
2471	
2472	// instrCoreConstraints is the shared core-constraints tail.
2473	const instrCoreConstraints = `
2474	
2475	## Constraints
2476	
2477	- Work only inside this execution worktree.
2478	- Keep ` + "`.ddx/executions/`" + ` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
2479	- **Never run ` + "`ddx init`" + `** — the workspace is initialized.
2480	- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
2481	- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.`
2482	
2483	// instrLongRunningMatrixGuards is the shared long-running matrix/benchmark guardrails (FEAT-010).
2484	const instrLongRunningMatrixGuards = `
2485	
2486	## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads
2487	
2488	For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):
2489	
2490	- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
2491	- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
2492	- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with ` + "`status: open`" + ` + retryable ` + "`reason`" + ` in ` + "`no_changes_rationale.txt`" + `. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.`
2493	
2494	// executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText is the <instructions> body used when the
2495	// harness carries its own rich system prompt (claude, codex, opencode,
2496	// unknown). It composes a Claude-specific preamble + process body with the
2497	// shared instr* blocks.
2498	const executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText = `You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.` +
2499		instrStep0SizeCheck +
2500		`
2501	
2502	## How to work
2503	
2504	- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
2505	- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
2506	- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
2507	- Run git/index mutations sequentially; do not use parallel tool calls for ` + "`git add`" + `, ` + "`git commit`" + `, or other staging/commit commands.
2508	- Stage with ` + "`git add <specific-paths>`" + `; never ` + "`git add -A`" + ` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
2509	- If ` + "`lefthook run pre-commit`" + ` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A ` + "`no-staged-files`" + ` run is not acceptance evidence.
2510	- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with ` + "`[<bead-id>]`" + `. Stop after the commit.
2511	- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
2512	- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run ` + "`ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`" + `, ` + "`ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`" + `, ` + "`ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`" + `, or ` + "`ddx bead close <bead-id>`" + `. Step 0 still allows ` + "`ddx bead create`" + `, ` + "`ddx bead dep add`" + `, and ` + "`ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`" + `.
2513	- If you cannot finish, write ` + "`no_changes_rationale.txt`" + ` under the bead metadata ` + "`bundle`" + ` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records ` + "`no_evidence_produced`" + `.` +
2514		instrNoChangesContract +
2515		instrInvestigationReports +
2516		instrBeadOverride +
2517		instrReviewGate +
2518		instrCoreConstraints +
2519		instrLongRunningMatrixGuards +
2520		`
2521	
2522	## When the work is done
2523	
2524	After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.`
2525	
2526	// executeBeadInstructionsAgentText is the <instructions> body used when the
2527	// harness selector routes to the embedded Fizeau agent (agent / fiz /
2528	// embedded). It composes an agent-specific preamble + tool-aware process
2529	// body with the shared instr* blocks. The Agent variant carries the

2531	const executeBeadInstructionsAgentText = `You are a coding agent executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. Tools: read, write, edit, bash, ls, grep, find. Use them, not ` + "`bash: cat`" + `, ` + "`bash: rg`" + `, or ` + "`bash: ls`" + `.
2532	
2533	The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract. Every AC must be satisfied by code here.` +
2534		instrStep0SizeCheck +
2535		`
2536	
2537	## Process
2538	
2539	- Read first. Read the files the bead names before editing — do not guess.
2540	- Use ` + "`edit`" + `, ` + "`write`" + `, ` + "`read`" + `, ` + "`grep`" + `, and ` + "`ls`" + `; never the bash equivalents.
2541	- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code**.
2542	- Run git/index mutations sequentially; do not use parallel tool calls for ` + "`git add`" + `, ` + "`git commit`" + `, or other staging/commit commands.
2543	- Stage with ` + "`git add <specific-paths>`" + `; never ` + "`git add -A`" + `.
2544	- If ` + "`lefthook run pre-commit`" + ` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A ` + "`no-staged-files`" + ` run is not acceptance evidence.
2545	- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with ` + "`[<bead-id>]`" + `.
2546	- Stop immediately after the commit succeeds. Do not keep reading or testing.
2547	- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
2548	- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run ` + "`ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`" + `, ` + "`ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`" + `, ` + "`ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`" + `, or ` + "`ddx bead close <bead-id>`" + `. Step 0 still allows ` + "`ddx bead create`" + `, ` + "`ddx bead dep add`" + `, and ` + "`ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`" + `.
2549	- If you cannot finish, write ` + "`no_changes_rationale.txt`" + ` under the bead metadata ` + "`bundle`" + ` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ ` + "`no_evidence_produced`" + `.` +
2550		instrNoChangesContract +
2551		instrInvestigationReports +
2552		instrBeadOverride +
2553		instrReviewGate +
2554		instrCoreConstraints +
2555		instrLongRunningMatrixGuards

2557	// executeBeadInstructionsText selects the right instructions variant for the
2558	// given harness. Harnesses with rich system prompts (claude, codex, opencode)
2559	// get the terser claude variant; the embedded Fizeau harness gets the
2560	// fuller agent variant with explicit tool names and stop-after-commit
2561	// scaffolding.
2562	func executeBeadInstructionsText(harness string) string {
2563		switch strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(harness)) {
2564		case "agent", "fiz", "embedded":
2565			return executeBeadInstructionsAgentText
2566		default:
2567			return executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText
2568		}
2569	}
