2571	// executeBeadMissingGoverningText is emitted inside <governing> when no
2572	// governing references were pre-resolved for the bead. The bead description
2573	// is the primary contract — this note only reminds the agent to treat it as
2574	// such and to ground any unclear decisions in repository state rather than
2575	// guessing.
2576	const executeBeadMissingGoverningText = `No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read them first. Ground decisions in repository state; do not guess.`
2577	
2578	func xmlEscape(s string) string {
2579		var buf bytes.Buffer
2580		_ = xml.EscapeText(&buf, []byte(s))
2581		return buf.String()
2582	}
2583	
2584	func xmlAttrEscape(s string) string {
2585		r := strings.NewReplacer(
2586			"&", "&",
2587			"<", "<",
2588			">", ">",
2589			"\"", """,
2590			"'", "'",
2591			"\n", "
",
2592			"\r", "",
2593			"\t", "	",
2594		)
2595		return r.Replace(s)
2596	}
2597	
2598	func buildPrompt(workDir string, b *bead.Bead, refs []executeBeadGoverningRef, artifacts *executeBeadArtifacts, baseRev, promptOverride, harness string, contextBudget string) ([]byte, string, error) {
2599		if strings.TrimSpace(promptOverride) != "" {
2600			path := promptOverride
2601			if !filepath.IsAbs(path) {
2602				path = filepath.Join(workDir, path)
2603			}
2604			data, err := readPromptFileBounded(path)
2605			if err != nil {
2606				return nil, "", fmt.Errorf("reading prompt override %q: %w", promptOverride, err)
2607			}
2608			return data, promptOverride, nil
2609		}
2610	
2611		var sb strings.Builder
2612		sb.WriteString("<execute-bead>\n")
2613	
2614		instructions := executeBeadInstructionsText(harness) + executeBeadDynamicStep0Hints(workDir, b)
2615		// Keep the initial prefix stable for provider prefix caches: the shared
2616		// instructions render first, bead-specific hints append at the end of that
2617		// block, and the attempt-specific bundle path only appears later on
2618		// <metadata bundle="..."/>.
2619		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <instructions>\n%s\n  </instructions>\n", xmlEscape(instructions))
2620	
2621		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <bead id=\"%s\">\n", xmlAttrEscape(b.ID))
2622		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <title>%s</title>\n", xmlEscape(strings.TrimSpace(b.Title)))
2623	
2624		desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description)
2625		if desc == "" {
2626			sb.WriteString("    <description/>\n")
2627		} else {
2628			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <description>\n%s\n    </description>\n", xmlEscape(desc))
2629		}
2630	
2631		acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance)
2632		if acc == "" {
2633			sb.WriteString("    <acceptance/>\n")
2634		} else {
2635			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <acceptance>\n%s\n    </acceptance>\n", xmlEscape(acc))
2636		}
2637	
2638		// Bead notes carry review findings from prior iterations, escalation
2639		// context, or operator hints that did not fit into the description at
2640		// creation time. Threading them into the prompt as a distinct section
2641		// lets the agent act on them without the operator having to rewrite the
2642		// description in place on every reopen.
2643		if notes := strings.TrimSpace(b.Notes); notes != "" {
2644			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <notes>\n%s\n    </notes>\n", xmlEscape(notes))
2645		}
2646	
2647		if len(b.Labels) > 0 {
2648			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <labels>%s</labels>\n", xmlEscape(strings.Join(b.Labels, ", ")))
2649		} else {
2650			sb.WriteString("    <labels/>\n")
2651		}
2652	
2653		metaAttrs := make([]string, 0, 6)
2654		if b.Parent != "" {
2655			metaAttrs = append(metaAttrs, fmt.Sprintf("parent=\"%s\"", xmlAttrEscape(b.Parent)))
2656		}
2657		if specID, _ := b.Extra["spec-id"].(string); strings.TrimSpace(specID) != "" {
2658			metaAttrs = append(metaAttrs, fmt.Sprintf("spec-id=\"%s\"", xmlAttrEscape(strings.TrimSpace(specID))))
2659		}
2660		metaAttrs = append(metaAttrs, fmt.Sprintf("base-rev=\"%s\"", xmlAttrEscape(baseRev)))
2661		metaAttrs = append(metaAttrs, fmt.Sprintf("bundle=\"%s\"", xmlAttrEscape(artifacts.DirRel)))
2662		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <metadata %s/>\n", strings.Join(metaAttrs, " "))
2663		sb.WriteString("  </bead>\n")
2664	
2665		// For minimal budget, omit full governing refs and only include bead metadata.
2666		// This significantly reduces prompt size for cheap-powerClass attempts on local models.
2667		if contextBudget == "minimal" {
2668			sb.WriteString("  <governing>\n    <note>No governing references.</note>\n  </governing>\n")
2669		} else {
2670			if len(refs) == 0 {
2671				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <governing>\n    <note>%s</note>\n  </governing>\n", xmlEscape(executeBeadMissingGoverningText))
2672			} else {
2673				sb.WriteString("  <governing>\n")
2674				for _, ref := range refs {
2675					attrs := fmt.Sprintf("id=\"%s\" path=\"%s\"", xmlAttrEscape(ref.ID), xmlAttrEscape(ref.Path))
2676					if strings.TrimSpace(ref.Title) == "" {
2677						fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref %s/>\n", attrs)
2678					} else {
2679						fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref %s>%s</ref>\n", attrs, xmlEscape(strings.TrimSpace(ref.Title)))
2680					}
2681				}
2682				sb.WriteString("  </governing>\n")
2683			}
2684		}
2685	
2686		sb.WriteString("</execute-bead>\n")
2687	
2688		return []byte(sb.String()), "synthesized", nil
2689	}
2690	
2691	const executeBeadLargeDescriptionHintThreshold = 8000
2692	const executeBeadAutoDecompositionDepthCap = 2
2693	
2694	func executeBeadDynamicStep0Hints(workDir string, b *bead.Bead) string {
2695		if b == nil {
2696			return ""
2697		}
2698	
2699		var sb strings.Builder
2700		desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description)
2701		if len(desc) > executeBeadLargeDescriptionHintThreshold {
2702			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "\n\n## Large description hint\n")
2703			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "This bead description is %d bytes (> %d). Treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and prefer child-bead scoping before implementation.\n",
2704				len(desc), executeBeadLargeDescriptionHintThreshold)
2705		}
2706	
2707		depth := beadDecompositionDepth(workDir, b)
2708		if depth >= executeBeadAutoDecompositionDepthCap {
2709			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "\n\n## Decomposition depth cap\n")
2710			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "This bead is already at decomposition depth %d. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.\n",
2711				depth)
2712		}
2713	
2714		return sb.String()
2715	}
2716	
2717	func beadDecompositionDepth(workDir string, b *bead.Bead) int {
2718		if b == nil {
2719			return 0
2720		}
2721	
2722		store := bead.NewStore(ddxroot.JoinProject(workDir))
2723		depth := beadDecomposedChildDepth(b)
2724		seen := map[string]struct{}{}
2725		current := b
2726		for current != nil {
2727			parentID := strings.TrimSpace(current.Parent)
2728			if parentID == "" {
2729				break
2730			}
2731			if _, ok := seen[parentID]; ok {
2732				break
2733			}
2734			seen[parentID] = struct{}{}
2735			parent, err := store.Get(context.Background(), parentID)
2736			if err != nil || parent == nil {
2737				break
2738			}
2739			if beadDecomposedChildDepth(parent) == 0 {
2740				break
2741			}
2742			depth++
2743			current = parent
2744		}
2745		return depth
2746	}
2747	
2748	func beadDecomposedChildDepth(b *bead.Bead) int {
2749		if b == nil || strings.TrimSpace(b.Parent) == "" {
2750			return 0
2751		}
2752		for _, label := range b.Labels {
2753			if strings.TrimSpace(label) == "decomposed" {
2754				return 1
2755			}
2756		}
2757		return 0
2758	}

2760	// promptSHA returns the hex-encoded sha256 of the rendered prompt bytes.
2761	// Used as manifest.prompt_sha so analytics can group attempts by the exact
2762	// prompt that produced them.
2763	func promptSHA(promptContent []byte) string {
2764		sum := sha256.Sum256(promptContent)
2765		return hex.EncodeToString(sum[:])
2766	}

2794	func executeBeadCacheHitRate(inputTokens, cachedTokens int) float64 {
2795		totalPromptInput := inputTokens + cachedTokens
2796		if cachedTokens <= 0 || totalPromptInput <= 0 {
2797			return 0
2798		}
2799		return float64(cachedTokens) / float64(totalPromptInput)
2800	}

2802	// VerifyCleanWorktree checks that the project root's working tree has no
2803	// untracked execution evidence files for the given attempt. If evidence files
2804	// remain (e.g. because the land flow did not commit them), it stages and
2805	// commits them as a safety net. Returns nil when the evidence dir is clean
2806	// or was successfully committed.
2807	func VerifyCleanWorktree(projectRoot, attemptID string) error {
2808		if attemptID == "" {
2809			return nil
2810		}
2811		evidenceDir := filepath.ToSlash(filepath.Join(ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID))
2812	
2813		out, _ := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "status", "--porcelain", "--", evidenceDir).Output()
2814		if len(strings.TrimSpace(string(out))) == 0 {
2815			return nil
2816		}
2817	
2818		// Exclude embedded session logs from the evidence commit; they stay
2819		// on disk for post-hoc inspection but must NOT be tracked — the
2820		// multi-thousand-line .jsonl files are what caused ddx-39e27896
2821		// (retry prompts ballooning past provider context limits).
2822		// manifest.json, result.json, prompt.md, usage.json remain tracked
2823		// per the existing audit-trail contract (gitignore un-ignores them).
2824		addArgs := append([]string{"add", "--", evidenceDir}, EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs()...)
2825		addOut, addErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, addArgs...).CombinedOutput()
2826		if addErr != nil {
2827			return fmt.Errorf("staging leftover evidence: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(addOut)), addErr)
2828		}
2829		diffOut, _ := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "diff", "--cached", "--name-only", "--", evidenceDir).Output()
2830		if len(strings.TrimSpace(string(diffOut))) == 0 {
2831			return nil
2832		}
2833		msg := fmt.Sprintf("chore: add execution evidence [%s]", shortAttempt(attemptID))
2834		commitOut, commitErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot,
2835			"-c", "user.name=ddx-land-coordinator",
2836			"-c", "user.email=coordinator@ddx.local",
2837			"commit", "-m", msg,
2838		).CombinedOutput()
2839		if commitErr != nil {
2840			return fmt.Errorf("committing leftover evidence: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(commitOut)), commitErr)
2841		}
2842		return nil
2843	}

2845	// commitEvidenceBundleInWorktree stages the evidence directory in the attempt
2846	// worktree and commits it so Land() can find the bundle inside the landing
2847	// finalization worktree without copying from the project root. Returns the new
2848	// HEAD SHA on success, or "" if staging finds nothing to commit (e.g. because
2849	// the worktree is not a real git repo in tests or staging fails).
2850	func commitEvidenceBundleInWorktree(wtPath, dirRel, attemptID string) string {
2851		if dirRel == "" {
2852			return ""
2853		}
2854		dirArg := filepath.FromSlash(dirRel)
2855		addArgs := append([]string{"add", "--", dirArg}, EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs()...)
2856		if _, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), wtPath, addArgs...).CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
2857			return ""
2858		}
2859		diffOut, _ := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), wtPath, "diff", "--cached", "--name-only", "--", dirArg).Output()
2860		if len(strings.TrimSpace(string(diffOut))) == 0 {
2861			return ""
2862		}
2863		msg := fmt.Sprintf("chore: add execution evidence [%s]", shortAttempt(attemptID))
2864		if _, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), wtPath,
2865			"-c", "user.name=ddx-land-coordinator",
2866			"-c", "user.email=coordinator@ddx.local",
2867			"commit", "--no-verify", "-m", msg,
2868		).CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
2869			return ""
2870		}
2871		headOut, headErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), wtPath, "rev-parse", "HEAD").Output()
2872		if headErr != nil {
2873			return ""
2874		}
2875		return strings.TrimSpace(string(headOut))
2876	}

2878	// publishEvidenceBundleToProjectRoot copies the evidence bundle from the
2879	// isolated attempt worktree to the project root. Used by non-landing paths
2880	// (no-changes, task-failed, operator-cancel) so that VerifyCleanWorktree can
2881	// commit the bundle. A no-op when the source does not exist or when wtPath and
2882	// projectRoot resolve to the same path.
2883	func publishEvidenceBundleToProjectRoot(projectRoot, wtPath, dirRel string) {
2884		if dirRel == "" || sameFilesystemPath(projectRoot, wtPath) {
2885			return
2886		}
2887		src := filepath.Join(wtPath, filepath.FromSlash(dirRel))
2888		dst := filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(dirRel))
2889		info, err := os.Stat(src)
2890		if err != nil || !info.IsDir() {
2891			return
2892		}
2893		_ = filepath.WalkDir(src, func(path string, d os.DirEntry, walkErr error) error {
2894			if walkErr != nil {
2895				return walkErr
2896			}
2897			rel, relErr := filepath.Rel(src, path)
2898			if relErr != nil {
2899				return relErr
2900			}
2901			target := filepath.Join(dst, rel)
2902			if d.IsDir() {
2903				return os.MkdirAll(target, 0o755)
2904			}
2905			data, readErr := os.ReadFile(path)
2906			if readErr != nil {
2907				return readErr
2908			}
2909			return os.WriteFile(target, data, 0o644)
2910		})
2911	}

2913	func excludeCleanupMetadataFromWorktreeGit(wtPath string) error {
2914		if _, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(wtPath, ".git")); err != nil {
2915			if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
2916				return nil
2917			}
2918			return err
2919		}
2920		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), wtPath, "rev-parse", "--git-path", "info/exclude").Output()
2921		if err != nil {
2922			return err
2923		}
2924		excludePath := strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
2925		if excludePath == "" {
2926			return fmt.Errorf("git exclude path is empty")
2927		}
2928		if !filepath.IsAbs(excludePath) {
2929			excludePath = filepath.Join(wtPath, excludePath)
2930		}
2931		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(excludePath), 0o755); err != nil {
2932			return err
2933		}
2934		existing, err := os.ReadFile(excludePath)
2935		if err == nil && strings.Contains(string(existing), "/"+ExecutionCleanupMetadataFileName) {
2936			return nil
2937		}
2938		if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
2939			return err
2940		}
2941		f, err := os.OpenFile(excludePath, os.O_CREATE|os.O_APPEND|os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
2942		if err != nil {
2943			return err
2944		}
2945		defer f.Close()
2946		if len(existing) > 0 && !strings.HasSuffix(string(existing), "\n") {
2947			if _, err := f.WriteString("\n"); err != nil {
2948				return err
2949			}
2950		}
2951		_, err = f.WriteString("/" + ExecutionCleanupMetadataFileName + "\n")
2952		return err
2953	}
2954	
2955	func writeArtifactJSON(path string, payload any) error {
2956		data, err := json.MarshalIndent(payload, "", "  ")
2957		if err != nil {
2958			return err
2959		}
2960		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
2961			return err
2962		}
2963		return os.WriteFile(path, data, 0o644)
2964	}

2966	// WriteExecuteBeadResultArtifact rewrites result.json in the project root with
2967	// the final worker/orchestrator result after landing or preservation. The
2968	// worker writes the initial task-level result inside the attempt worktree;
2969	// callers use this helper after ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult so the
2970	// durable evidence bundle does not continue to report worker success when the
2971	// orchestrator preserved the candidate.
2972	func WriteExecuteBeadResultArtifact(projectRoot string, res *ExecuteBeadResult) error {
2973		if res == nil {
2974			return nil
2975		}
2976		resultFile := strings.TrimSpace(res.ResultFile)
2977		if resultFile == "" && strings.TrimSpace(res.ExecutionDir) != "" {
2978			resultFile = filepath.ToSlash(filepath.Join(res.ExecutionDir, "result.json"))
2979		}
2980		if resultFile == "" {
2981			return nil
2982		}
2983		path := filepath.FromSlash(resultFile)
2984		if !filepath.IsAbs(path) {
2985			path = filepath.Join(projectRoot, path)
2986		}
2987		return writeArtifactJSON(path, res)
2988	}

140	// ExecutionCleanupLivenessProbe decides whether a cleanup candidate should be
141	// preserved because it is still live.
142	type ExecutionCleanupLivenessProbe interface {
143		IsLive(meta ExecutionCleanupMetadata, runState *RunState, now time.Time) (bool, string)
144	}
145	
146	// defaultExecutionCleanupLivenessProbe preserves preserved metadata, a temp
147	// execution directory referenced by the live run-state file, or a candidate
148	// with an unexpired liveness marker. Tests can override the probe to model
149	// more specific host behavior.
150	type defaultExecutionCleanupLivenessProbe struct{}
151	
152	func (defaultExecutionCleanupLivenessProbe) IsLive(meta ExecutionCleanupMetadata, runState *RunState, now time.Time) (bool, string) {
153		if meta.Preserved {
154			return true, "preserved metadata"
155		}
156		if meta.ActiveCandidateCycle {
157			return true, candidateCycleRecoveryReason(meta, "active candidate cycle")
158		}
159		if strings.TrimSpace(meta.CandidateRef) != "" {
160			return true, candidateCycleRecoveryReason(meta, "candidate ref recovery")
161		}
162		if runState != nil {
163			if strings.TrimSpace(runState.CandidateCyclePhase) != "" {
164				return true, candidateCycleRecoveryReason(candidateCycleMetadataFromRunState(*runState), "active candidate cycle")
165			}
166			if meta.WorktreePath != "" && filepath.Clean(runState.WorktreePath) == filepath.Clean(meta.WorktreePath) {
167				return true, "matched live run-state"
168			}
169			if meta.AttemptID != "" && runState.AttemptID == meta.AttemptID {
170				return true, "matched live run-state"
171			}
172		}
173		if meta.Liveness != nil {
174			if meta.Liveness.PID > 0 && trackerProcessAlive(meta.Liveness.PID) {
175				return true, "live pid"
176			}
177			if !meta.Liveness.ExpiresAt.IsZero() && now.Before(meta.Liveness.ExpiresAt) {
178				return true, "unexpired liveness"
179			}

Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me provide a comprehensive summary of the DDx CLI's agent package structure, especially around worktree management and bead execution.

## Summary: DDx CLI Agent Package Structure - Worktree Management & Bead Execution

### Overall Architecture

The DDx CLI uses a **three-layer execution model** for bead execution:

1. **Layer 1 (CLI)**: `cmd/try.go` and `cmd/work.go` - Entry points for `ddx try` (single bead) and `ddx work` (queue drain)
2. **Layer 2**: `internal/agent/` - Worker executor, orchestration, and worktree management
3. **Layer 3**: Attempt backends (worktree, clone, docker) - Isolated execution environments

---

### Core Files & Their Roles

#### **Bead Execution Entry Points**

- **`/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/cmd/try.go`** (623 lines)
  - Executes a single targeted bead through the full production pipeline
  - Performs pre-flight checks: bead exists, is claimable, dependencies met
  - Routes through `agent.ExecuteBeadWorker` with custom `singleBeadStore` to bypass cooldown if `--force-claim` specified
  - Exit codes: 0 (success/satisfied), 1 (preserved/no-changes), 2 (failed)

- **`/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/cmd/work.go`** (363 lines)
  - Drains the execution-ready queue via `runAgentExecuteLoopImpl`
  - Treats harness/model/provider as opaque passthrough constraints
  - Implements retry-power policy (min-power/max-power bounds)
  - Stop conditions: drained, blocked, deferred (budget), no_progress, signal, operator_attention

#### **Core Execution Logic**

- **`/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`** (2989 lines - VERY LARGE)
  - **Main Function**: `ExecuteBeadWithConfig()` - the thin worker that:
    1. Resolves attempt backend (worktree/clone/docker/in-tree)
    2. Creates isolated workspace via `attemptBackend.Prepare()`
    3. Synthesizes execution prompt and manifest
    4. Runs agent via `attemptBackend.Run()` 
    5. Commits evidence bundle
    6. Classifies outcome (task_succeeded/task_failed/task_no_changes/task_no_evidence)
    7. Cleanup via `attemptBackend.Cleanup()`

  - **RealGitOps interface** - git operations on worktrees:
    - `WorktreeAdd(dir, wtPath, rev)` - Creates isolated worktree with `git worktree add --detach`
    - `WorktreeRemove(dir, wtPath)` - Removes worktree with `git worktree remove --force`
    - `WorktreeList(dir)` - Lists registered worktrees via `git worktree list --porcelain`
    - `WorktreePrune(dir)` - Prunes stale worktree records via `git worktree prune`
    - `SynthesizeCommit(dir, msg)` - Stages real changes (excluding noise paths) and commits

  - **Worktree Path Construction**:
    ```
    `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-<bead-id>-<attempt-id>`
    Located in: config.ExecutionTempRoot(projectRoot)
    ```

  - **Pre-dispatch Sequence** (executed under tracker lock):
    1. `commitTrackerLocked()` - Commit beads.jsonl if dirty
    2. `checkpointPreDispatchDirt()` - Preserve caller dirt as commits
    3. `resolveBase()` - Get base revision
    4. `attemptBackend.Prepare()` - Create workspace

  - **Evidence Bundle**:
    - Location: `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` (inside worktree)
    - Contains: prompt.md, manifest.json, result.json, usage.json, checks.json
    - Committed separately from agent's code commit (via `commitEvidenceBundleInWorktree()`)

---

### Attempt Backend System

**File**: `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go` (400+ lines)

**Interface** (`AttemptBackend`):
```go
type AttemptBackend interface {
    Name() string
    Prepare(ctx, req) (*AttemptWorkspace, error)  // Create workspace
    Run(ctx, req) (*Result, error)                // Run agent
    PublishResult(ctx, ws, res) error             // Mirror results
    Cleanup(ctx, ws) error                        // Clean up
}
```

**Implementations**:

1. **WorktreeAttemptBackend** (default)
   - Uses `git worktree add --detach <wtPath> <baseRev>`
   - Cleanup: `git worktree remove --force <wtPath>`
   - Path: `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-<bead-id>-<attempt-id>`
   - Fast (shared object store), isolates worktree state

2. **LocalCloneAttemptBackend**
   - Uses `git clone --local <projectRoot> <clonePath>` 
   - Optional hardlink mode with fallback to no-hardlinks
   - Cleanup: `rm -rf <clonePath>`
   - Heavier than worktree but more isolated

3. **DockerCloneAttemptBackend**
   - Wraps LocalCloneAttemptBackend inside Docker container
   - Prepares Docker home (~/.docker-home-*) and runtime dirs
   - Cleanup: Removes clone + Docker dirs

4. **InTreeAttemptBackend**
   - No isolation - executes in project root with file locking
   - For development/testing only

---

### Worktree Lifecycle

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 1. PREPARE PHASE (under tracker lock)                   │
│    - commitTrackerLocked()                              │
│    - checkpointPreDispatchDirt()                        │
│    - attemptBackend.Prepare() → WorktreeAttemptBackend │
│      └─> git worktree add --detach <wtPath> <baseRev>  │
│      └─> ExcludeCleanupMetadataFromWorktreeGit()       │
│      └─> WriteExecutionCleanupMetadata(cleanup.json)   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                            ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 2. EXECUTION PHASE (isolated, parallel-safe)            │
│    - materializeWorktreeSkills() (fix broken symlinks)  │
│    - prepareArtifacts() (load context, build prompt)    │
│    - prepareExecuteBeadGitIsolation() (git config)      │
│    - attemptBackend.Run() → dispatchAgentRun()          │
│      └─> Agent subprocess executes in wtPath            │
│      └─> startRunStateRefresh() polls liveness          │
│      └─> startCancelPoll() for operator cancel SLA      │
│    - SynthesizeCommit() if dirty (exclude noise paths)  │
│    - commitEvidenceBundleInWorktree()                   │
│    - MirrorOrLog() (optional out-of-band mirror)        │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                            ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 3. CLEANUP PHASE (deferred, runs last)                  │
│    - publishEvidenceBundleToProjectRoot() (if needed)   │
│    - attemptBackend.Cleanup() → WorktreeRemove         │
│      └─> git worktree remove --force <wtPath>          │
│      └─> git worktree prune                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Critical Detail**: Cleanup runs in a deferred block registered **BEFORE** other defers, so it runs **LAST** in LIFO order (lines 1567-1583, execute_bead.go).

---

### Git Isolation & Configuration

**File**: `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_git_isolation.go`

The worker prepares isolated git config for the worktree:

1. **Enable worktree-specific config**: `git config extensions.worktreeConfig true`
2. **Create git wrapper script** (PATH override):
   - Intercepts `git config` calls
   - Blocks dangerous keys: `core.bare`, `core.worktree`
   - Routes config to private scope via `GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL` and custom private config
3. **Seed worktree config**: Copy main repo config to `config.worktree`

This prevents the agent from accidentally breaking the worktree setup via git config mutations.

---

### Cleanup & Metadata Management

**File**: `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go`

Each worktree carries `cleanup.json` metadata:
```json
{
  "project_root": "/path/to/project",
  "bead_id": "ddx-abc123",
  "attempt_id": "20260531T020844-6b1791a4",
  "worktree_path": ".../.execute-bead-wt-...",
  "registered": true,
  "active_candidate_cycle": false,
  "candidate_cycle_phase": "",
  "liveness": {
    "pid": 12345,
    "refreshed_at": "2026-05-31T02:08:44Z",
    "expires_at": "2026-05-31T02:09:04Z"
  }
}
```

**ExecutionCleanupManager** decides whether to preserve or remove worktrees based on:
- Registered state + liveness probe
- Active candidate cycle flag
- Live run-state matching (PIDs, attempt IDs)
- Candidate ref recovery (for post-review inspection)
- PID liveness + freshness timeout (default 2 min)

---

### Result Classification

Output outcomes (ExecuteBeadResult.Outcome):
- `task_succeeded` - Agent exited 0, produced commits
- `task_failed` - Agent exited non-zero OR validation failed
- `task_no_changes` - Agent exited 0, no commits, wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt`
- `task_no_evidence` - Agent exited 0, no commits, no rationale (ERROR condition)

Status values (ExecuteBeadResult.Status):
- `execution_success` / `already_satisfied` - Won't be merged (already done)
- `execution_failed` - Preserved for review or needs human intervention
- `no_changes` / `no_evidence_produced` - No changes made
- `resource_exhausted` - Disk/host limit hit
- `worktree_lost` - Catastrophic: worktree vanished mid-execution

---

### Key Files Summary

| File | LOC | Purpose |
|------|-----|---------|
| `execute_bead.go` | 2989 | Core execution worker & git operations |
| `attempt_backend.go` | 400+ | Backend interface & worktree/clone/docker impls |
| `execute_bead_git_isolation.go` | 200+ | Git config isolation wrapper |
| `execution_cleanup.go` | 300+ | Cleanup metadata & liveness probe |
| `worktree_skills.go` | 61 | Repair broken symlinks in worktree |
| `cmd/try.go` | 623 | Single-bead executor |
| `cmd/work.go` | 363 | Queue drain + retry policy |

---

### Current State: No Explicit Worktree Pooling

**Important Finding**: The codebase **does NOT pool or reuse worktrees**. Each `execute-bead` attempt:
1. Creates a fresh worktree via `git worktree add`
2. Executes the agent
3. Commits evidence
4. Immediately cleans up via `git worktree remove --force`

Worktrees are cleaned on **success** (KeepOnError=false) but preserved on **execution failure** for operator inspection via the cleanup metadata system.

---

### Testing

263 total .go files in the agent package, including:
- `execute_bead_*_test.go` - Comprehensive integration tests
- `attempt_backend_test.go` - Backend behavior tests
- `execution_cleanup_test.go` - Cleanup lifecycle tests
- `worktree_skills_test.go` - Symlink repair tests

21	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
22	// Git helpers
23	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
24	
25	// scrubbedGitEnvInteg returns the current environment with all GIT_* variables
26	// removed, ensuring test-local git subprocesses don't inherit parent repo state.
27	func scrubbedGitEnvInteg() []string {
28		parent := os.Environ()
29		env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
30		for _, kv := range parent {
31			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
32				continue
33			}
34			env = append(env, kv)
35		}
36		return env
37	}
38	
39	// runGitInteg runs a git command in dir with scrubbed GIT_* env.
40	// Fails the test on non-zero exit.
41	func runGitInteg(t *testing.T, dir string, args ...string) string {
42		t.Helper()
43		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
44		cmd.Dir = dir
45		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
46		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
47		if err != nil {
48			t.Fatalf("git %v in %s: %v\n%s", args, dir, err, out)
49		}
50		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
51	}
52	
53	// runGitIntegOutput runs a git command and returns (output, error) — for cases
54	// where failure is expected or handled by the caller.
55	func runGitIntegOutput(dir string, args ...string) (string, error) {
56		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
57		cmd.Dir = dir
58		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
59		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
60		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err
61	}

63	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
64	// Repo setup
65	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
66	
67	// newScriptHarnessRepo creates a temp git repo with an initial seed commit on
68	// main, seeds .ddx/beads.jsonl with beadCount open beads (IDs like
69	// ddx-int-0001, ddx-int-0002 …), and returns (projectRoot, initialSHA).
70	// The initialSHA is the SHA immediately after the "chore: initial seed" commit,
71	// BEFORE the seed-beads commit, so callers can measure commits added beyond it.
72	func newScriptHarnessRepo(t *testing.T, beadCount int) (string, string) {
73		t.Helper()
74	
75		setExecutionWorktreeRootForTest(t)
76		root := t.TempDir()
77	
78		runGitInteg(t, root, "init", "-b", "main")
79		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
80		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
81	
82		// Create initial seed file and commit so the repo has a HEAD.
83		seedFile := filepath.Join(root, "seed.txt")
84		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(seedFile, []byte("seed\n"), 0644))
85		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".")
86		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
87	
88		initialSHA := runGitInteg(t, root, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
89	
90		// Set up .ddx dir and bead store.
91		ddxDir := filepath.Join(root, ddxroot.DirName)
92		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
93		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
94		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
95	
96		for i := 0; i < beadCount; i++ {
97			id := fmt.Sprintf("ddx-int-%04d", i+1)
98			b := &bead.Bead{
99				ID:        id,
100				Title:     fmt.Sprintf("Integration test bead %d", i+1),
101				IssueType: "task",
102				Priority:  i,
103			}
104			require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
105		}
106	
107		// Commit beads.jsonl so the initial worktree contains it.
108		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
109		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: seed beads")
110	
111		return root, initialSHA
112	}

117		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(root, 0o755))
118		t.Setenv(config.ExecutionWorktreeRootEnv, root)

127	func writeDirectiveFile(t *testing.T, path string, lines []string) {

134	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
135	// Executor helpers
136	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
137	
138	// landSerializerMu guards landSerializerMap.
139	var (
140		landSerializerMu  sync.Mutex
141		landSerializerMap = map[string]*sync.Mutex{}
142	)
143	
144	// landMutexFor returns the per-projectRoot mutex that serializes Land() calls.
145	// This mimics the production per-project LandCoordinator goroutine
146	// (cli/internal/server/workers.go). Tests that spawn concurrent workers share
147	// this mutex so the CAS-based UpdateRefTo in Land() never races.
148	func landMutexFor(projectRoot string) *sync.Mutex {
149		landSerializerMu.Lock()
150		defer landSerializerMu.Unlock()
151		m, ok := landSerializerMap[projectRoot]
152		if !ok {
153			m = &sync.Mutex{}
154			landSerializerMap[projectRoot] = m
155		}
156		return m
157	}
158	
159	// scriptHarnessExecutor builds an ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc that:
160	//  1. Runs ExecuteBead with the script harness (directive file = directivePath).
161	//  2. Passes the result through LandBeadResult with a LandingAdvancer that
162	//     calls Land() — serialized via landMutexFor(projectRoot).
163	//
164	// Both ExecuteBead and Land() are serialized per projectRoot. This is required
165	// because ExecuteBead calls CommitTracker (which acquires the git index lock)
166	// and because Land() uses CAS-based UpdateRefTo. In production, the server
167	// dispatches one bead per worker and serializes Land() via the LandCoordinator
168	// goroutine; in integration tests, the same mutex handles both.
169	//
170	// Failed exits (exit code != 0) with commits are preserved under
171	// refs/ddx/iterations/ by LandBeadResult before Land() is invoked.
172	func scriptHarnessExecutor(t *testing.T, projectRoot, directivePath string) ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc {
173		t.Helper()
174		// Build Runner once at executor-construction time so concurrent goroutine
175		// calls share a stable test runner instance.
176		runner := NewRunner(Config{})
177		gitOps := &RealGitOps{}
178		orchGitOps := &RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
179		// Per-projectRoot mutex serializes git operations so concurrent workers
180		// don't race on the git index or on Land()'s CAS UpdateRefTo.
181		repoMu := landMutexFor(projectRoot)
182	
183		cfg := config.NewTestConfigForBead(config.TestBeadConfigOpts{
184			Model: directivePath,
185		})
186		rcfg := cfg.Resolve(config.CLIOverrides{Harness: "script"})
187	
188		return ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
189			repoMu.Lock()
190			defer repoMu.Unlock()
191	
192			res, err := ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx, projectRoot, beadID, rcfg, ExecuteBeadRuntime{
193				AgentRunner: runner,
194			}, gitOps)
195			if err != nil {
196				if res != nil {
197					return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
198				}
199				return ExecuteBeadReport{
200					BeadID: beadID,
201					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
202					Detail: err.Error(),
203				}, nil
204			}
205	
206			landFromRev := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
207			landing, landErr := LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, orchGitOps, BeadLandingOptions{
208				LandingAdvancer: func(r *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error) {
209					// landMutexFor is already held (repoMu == landMutexFor(projectRoot)).
210					req := BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, r)
211					return Land(projectRoot, req, RealLandingGitOps{})
212				},
213			})
214			if landErr != nil {
215				return ExecuteBeadReport{
216					BeadID: beadID,
217					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
218					Detail: landErr.Error(),
219				}, nil
220			}
221			ApplyLandingToResult(res, landing)
222			if syncErr := (RealLandingGitOps{}).SyncWorkTreeToHead(projectRoot, landFromRev); syncErr != nil {
223				return ExecuteBeadReport{
224					BeadID: beadID,
225					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
226					Detail: syncErr.Error(),
227				}, nil
228			}
229			return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
230		})
231	}
232	
233	// executeBeadResultToReport converts ExecuteBeadResult to ExecuteBeadReport.
234	func executeBeadResultToReport(res *ExecuteBeadResult) ExecuteBeadReport {
235		return ExecuteBeadReport{
236			BeadID:             res.BeadID,
237			AttemptID:          res.AttemptID,
238			WorkerID:           res.WorkerID,
239			Harness:            res.Harness,
240			Model:              res.Model,
241			Status:             res.Status,
242			Detail:             res.Detail,
243			SessionID:          res.SessionID,
244			BaseRev:            res.BaseRev,
245			ResultRev:          res.ResultRev,
246			PreserveRef:        res.PreserveRef,
247			NoChangesRationale: res.NoChangesRationale,
248			Error:              res.Error,
249			Stderr:             res.Stderr,
250			RateLimitBudget:    res.RateLimitBudget,
251		}
252	}

257	
258	// gitCommitCount returns the number of commits reachable from ref (plus any
259	// additional git rev-list args like "--not", "SHA").
260	func gitCommitCount(t *testing.T, projectRoot string, refAndArgs ...string) int {
261		t.Helper()
262		n, err := gitCommitCountOutput(projectRoot, refAndArgs...)
263		require.NoError(t, err, "git rev-list --count %v", refAndArgs)
264		return n
265	}
266	
267	func gitCommitCountOutput(projectRoot string, refAndArgs ...string) (int, error) {
268		args := append([]string{"rev-list", "--count"}, refAndArgs...)
269		out, err := runGitIntegOutput(projectRoot, args...)
270		if err != nil {
271			return 0, fmt.Errorf("git %v in %s: %w (%s)", args, projectRoot, err, out)
272		}
273		n, err := strconv.Atoi(out)
274		if err != nil {
275			return 0, fmt.Errorf("parse git rev-list count %q: %w", out, err)
276		}
277		return n, nil
278	}

284	// makeLoopStore creates an ExecuteBeadLoopStore backed by a bead.Store rooted
285	// in ddxDir. The store is already initialised by newScriptHarnessRepo.

306	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

309	
310	// escapeForShell escapes a string for embedding in a single-quoted shell argument.

314	
315	func requireConcurrentScriptHarnessLandingInvariant(
316		t *testing.T,
317		projectRoot, ddxDir, initialSHA string,
318		results []*ExecuteBeadLoopResult,
319		errs []error,
320		beadCount int,
321	) {
322		t.Helper()
323	
324		failures := make([]string, 0)
325		totalAttempts := 0
326		totalSuccesses := 0
327		executedByBead := make(map[string]int, beadCount)
328	
329		if len(results) != beadCount {
330			failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("worker result count=%d, want %d", len(results), beadCount))
331		}
332		if len(errs) != beadCount {
333			failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("worker error count=%d, want %d", len(errs), beadCount))
334		}
335	
336		for i, err := range errs {
337			if err != nil {
338				failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("worker %d returned error: %v", i, err))
339			}
340		}
341	
342		for i, result := range results {
343			if result == nil {
344				failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("worker %d returned nil loop result", i))
345				continue
346			}
347			totalAttempts += result.Attempts
348			totalSuccesses += result.Successes
349			for _, report := range result.Results {
350				executedByBead[report.BeadID]++
351				if report.Status != ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {
352					failures = append(failures,
353						fmt.Sprintf("worker %d reported bead %s status=%s detail=%q", i, report.BeadID, report.Status, report.Detail))
354				}
355			}
356		}
357	
358		if totalAttempts != beadCount {
359			failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("total attempts=%d, want %d", totalAttempts, beadCount))
360		}
361		if totalSuccesses != beadCount {
362			failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("total successes=%d, want %d", totalSuccesses, beadCount))
363		}
364	
365		for i := 1; i <= beadCount; i++ {
366			beadID := fmt.Sprintf("ddx-int-%04d", i)
367			if got := executedByBead[beadID]; got != 1 {
368				failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("bead %s executed %d times, want exactly once", beadID, got))
369			}
370		}
371	
372		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
373		all, err := store.List("", "", nil)
374		if err != nil {
375			failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("list beads: %v", err))
376		} else {
377			byID := make(map[string]bead.Bead, len(all))
378			closedCount := 0
379			for _, item := range all {
380				byID[item.ID] = item
381				if item.Status == bead.StatusClosed {
382					closedCount++
383				}
384			}
385			if len(all) != beadCount {
386				failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("bead count=%d, want %d", len(all), beadCount))
387			}
388			if closedCount != beadCount {
389				failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("closed bead count=%d, want %d", closedCount, beadCount))
390			}
391			for i := 1; i <= beadCount; i++ {
392				beadID := fmt.Sprintf("ddx-int-%04d", i)
393				item, ok := byID[beadID]
394				if !ok {
395					failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("missing bead row for %s", beadID))
396					continue
397				}
398				if item.Status != bead.StatusClosed {
399					failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("bead %s status=%s, want closed", beadID, item.Status))
400				}
401				if closingSHA := extraString(item.Extra, "closing_commit_sha"); closingSHA == "" {
402					failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("bead %s missing closing_commit_sha", beadID))
403				}
404			}
405		}
406	
407		commitsOnMain, err := gitCommitCountOutput(projectRoot, "HEAD", "--not", initialSHA)
408		if err != nil {
409			failures = append(failures, fmt.Sprintf("count commits on main: %v", err))
410		} else if commitsOnMain < beadCount {
411			failures = append(failures,
412				fmt.Sprintf("commits on main since seed=%d, want at least %d", commitsOnMain, beadCount))
413		}
414	
415		for i := 1; i <= beadCount; i++ {
416			fileName := fmt.Sprintf("bead-%04d.txt", i)
417			out, err := runGitIntegOutput(projectRoot, "show", "HEAD:"+fileName)
418			if err != nil {
419				failures = append(failures,
420					fmt.Sprintf("HEAD:%s not reachable: %v (%s)", fileName, err, strings.TrimSpace(out)))
421			}
422		}
423	
424		if len(failures) == 0 {
425			return
426		}
427	
428		t.Fatalf("concurrent script-harness landing invariant failed:\n- %s\n\nDiagnostics:\n%s",
429			strings.Join(failures, "\n- "),
430			formatConcurrentLandingDiagnostics(projectRoot, ddxDir, results, errs),
431		)
432	}
433	
434	func formatConcurrentLandingDiagnostics(projectRoot, ddxDir string, results []*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, errs []error) string {
435		var b strings.Builder
436	
437		b.WriteString("workers:\n")
438		for i := 0; i < maxLandingDiagInt(len(results), len(errs)); i++ {
439			var (
440				result *ExecuteBeadLoopResult
441				err    error
442			)
443			if i < len(results) {
444				result = results[i]
445			}
446			if i < len(errs) {
447				err = errs[i]
448			}
449			b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("  worker-%d err=%v", i, err))
450			if result == nil {
451				b.WriteString(" result=<nil>\n")
452				continue
453			}
454			b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(
455				" attempts=%d successes=%d failures=%d stop=%q exit=%q no_ready=%t no_ready_detail=%+v\n",
456				result.Attempts,
457				result.Successes,
458				result.Failures,
459				result.StopCondition,
460				result.ExitReason,
461				result.NoReadyWork,
462				result.NoReadyWorkDetail,
463			))
464			for _, report := range result.Results {
465				b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(
466					"    bead=%s status=%s detail=%q attempt=%s result_rev=%s landed_rev=%s preserve_ref=%s\n",
467					report.BeadID,
468					report.Status,
469					report.Detail,
470					report.AttemptID,
471					report.ResultRev,
472					report.LandedRev,
473					report.PreserveRef,
474				))
475			}
476		}
477	
478		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
479		all, err := store.List("", "", nil)
480		if err != nil {
481			b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("beads: list error: %v\n", err))
482		} else {
483			sort.Slice(all, func(i, j int) bool { return all[i].ID < all[j].ID })
484			closedCount := 0
485			b.WriteString("bead lifecycle summary:\n")
486			for _, item := range all {
487				if item.Status == bead.StatusClosed {
488					closedCount++
489				}
490				events, eventsErr := store.Events(item.ID)
491				lastKinds := ""
492				if eventsErr != nil {
493					lastKinds = "events-error:" + eventsErr.Error()
494				} else {
495					lastKinds = tailEventKinds(events, 3)
496				}
497				b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(
498					"  %s status=%s owner=%s closing_commit_sha=%s work_last_status=%s events=%d last_events=%s\n",
499					item.ID,
500					item.Status,
501					item.Owner,
502					extraString(item.Extra, "closing_commit_sha"),
503					extraString(item.Extra, "work-last-status"),
504					len(events),
505					lastKinds,
506				))
507			}
508			b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("  closed_count=%d total=%d\n", closedCount, len(all)))
509			if breakdown, breakdownErr := store.ReadyExecutionBreakdown(); breakdownErr != nil {
510				b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("queue breakdown error: %v\n", breakdownErr))
511			} else {
512				b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(
513					"queue breakdown: ready=%v dependency_waiting=%v proposed=%v retry_cooldown=%v external_blocked=%v not_eligible=%v superseded=%v epics=%v epic_closure_candidates=%v next_retry_after=%s human_review_blocked_total=%d\n",
514					breakdown.ExecutionReady,
515					breakdown.DependencyWaiting,
516					breakdown.ProposedOperatorAttention,
517					breakdown.RetryCooldown,
518					breakdown.ExternalBlocked,
519					breakdown.NotEligible,
520					breakdown.Superseded,
521					breakdown.Epics,
522					breakdown.EpicClosureCandidates,
523					breakdown.NextRetryAfter,
524					breakdown.HumanReviewBlockedTotal,
525				))
526			}
527		}
528	
529		b.WriteString("git refs:\n")
530		head, headErr := runGitIntegOutput(projectRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
531		b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("  HEAD=%s err=%v\n", strings.TrimSpace(head), headErr))
532		refs, refsErr := runGitIntegOutput(projectRoot, "for-each-ref", "--format=%(refname) %(objectname)", "refs/heads", "refs/ddx/iterations")
533		if refsErr != nil {
534			b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("  for-each-ref error: %v\n", refsErr))
535		} else if strings.TrimSpace(refs) == "" {
536			b.WriteString("  (no refs)\n")
537		} else {
538			for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(refs), "\n") {
539				b.WriteString("  " + line + "\n")
540			}
541		}
542	
543		return b.String()
544	}
545	
546	func extraString(extra map[string]any, key string) string {
547		if extra == nil {
548			return ""
549		}
550		raw, ok := extra[key]
551		if !ok || raw == nil {
552			return ""
553		}
554		return strings.TrimSpace(fmt.Sprint(raw))
555	}
556	
557	func tailEventKinds(events []bead.BeadEvent, n int) string {
558		if len(events) == 0 {
559			return "(none)"
560		}
561		if n > len(events) {
562			n = len(events)
563		}
564		kinds := make([]string, 0, n)
565		for _, event := range events[len(events)-n:] {
566			kinds = append(kinds, event.Kind)
567		}
568		return strings.Join(kinds, ",")
569	}
570	
571	func maxLandingDiagInt(a, b int) int {
572		if a > b {
573			return a
574		}
575		return b
576	}

17	// LockRetryPolicy describes the retry/backoff curve used when attempting to
18	// acquire a process-shared lock that guards git index operations on the
19	// primary .git (the tracker-commit lock). The policy is owned by this
20	// package per ddx-da11a34a AC#4: callers in cli/internal/triage and
21	// elsewhere consult ActionRetryWithBackoff but do not duplicate the curve
22	// implementation here.
23	//
24	// The curve is exponential with a cap and a bounded max-retries budget so
25	// that lock contention is absorbed silently up to a point, after which it
26	// surfaces as an ordinary error to the caller. Per TD-031 §8.5
27	// (LockContentionContract) lock contention does NOT change persisted bead
28	// status; the bead remains claimable and the worker remains `draining`.
29	type LockRetryPolicy struct {
30		// InitialBackoff is the wait before the second attempt.
31		InitialBackoff time.Duration
32		// MaxBackoff caps the per-step backoff after exponential growth.
33		MaxBackoff time.Duration
34		// Multiplier is the per-step growth factor (>= 1.0).
35		Multiplier float64
36		// MaxRetries bounds the number of acquisition attempts after the
37		// initial try. Total attempts = MaxRetries + 1.
38		MaxRetries int
39		// MaxElapsed bounds the total wall-clock time spent retrying. If both
40		// MaxRetries and MaxElapsed are set, the first to trip wins.
41		MaxElapsed time.Duration
42	}
43	
44	// DefaultLockRetryPolicy returns the policy used by withTrackerLock. The
45	// curve is intentionally tight (sub-second) for in-process tracker-commit
46	// contention; the larger 30s/90s/300s curve referenced by the auto-triage
47	// child of ddx-3c154349 lives one layer up, in cli/internal/triage —
48	// it consults ActionRetryWithBackoff and re-queues across drain passes,
49	// while this policy resolves contention within a single attempt.
50	func DefaultLockRetryPolicy() LockRetryPolicy {
51		return LockRetryPolicy{
52			InitialBackoff: 50 * time.Millisecond,
53			MaxBackoff:     1 * time.Second,
54			Multiplier:     2.0,
55			MaxRetries:     200,
56			MaxElapsed:     30 * time.Second,
57		}
58	}
59	
60	// step returns the backoff duration for retry index n (0-based: n=0 is the
61	// wait BEFORE the second acquisition attempt).
62	func (p LockRetryPolicy) step(n int) time.Duration {
63		if p.InitialBackoff <= 0 {
64			return 0
65		}
66		d := float64(p.InitialBackoff)
67		m := p.Multiplier
68		if m < 1 {
69			m = 1
70		}
71		for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
72			d *= m
73			if p.MaxBackoff > 0 && d >= float64(p.MaxBackoff) {
74				d = float64(p.MaxBackoff)
75				break
76			}
77		}
78		out := time.Duration(d)
79		if p.MaxBackoff > 0 && out > p.MaxBackoff {
80			out = p.MaxBackoff
81		}
82		return out
83	}
84	
85	// trackerLockStaleAge is the age after which a held tracker lock is
86	// considered stale (owner crashed mid-commit) and may be forcibly broken.
87	var trackerLockStaleAge = 10 * time.Minute
88	
89	// trackerLockPolicy is the active retry policy for withTrackerLock.
90	// Tests may swap this out via the exported helpers below.
91	var trackerLockPolicy = DefaultLockRetryPolicy()
92	
93	// trackerLockPath returns the main-git lock directory for the given project
94	// root. The historical name remains because tests and older comments refer to
95	// the tracker lock, but the lock now protects all primary-checkout git writes:
96	// tracker commits, pre-dispatch checkpoint/ref updates, and landing.
97	//
98	// The lock target must be shared across linked worktrees for the same repo, so
99	// we prefer the primary DDx workspace when one exists instead of the caller's
100	// checkout-local .ddx directory.
101	func trackerLockPath(projectRoot string) string {
102		return filepath.Join(sharedMainGitLockRoot(projectRoot), ddxroot.DirName, ".git-tracker.lock")
103	}
104	
105	// sharedMainGitLockRoot resolves the DDx state root that should own the
106	// process-shared main-git lock. Linked worktrees should converge on the same
107	// primary workspace when one is available; otherwise we fall back to the
108	// normal project-scoped DDx path so convention-root projects keep their
109	// existing behavior.
110	func sharedMainGitLockRoot(projectRoot string) string {
111		if workspace := gitpkg.FindNearestDDxWorkspace(projectRoot); workspace != "" {
112			if info, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(workspace, ddxroot.DirName)); err == nil && info.IsDir() {
113				return workspace
114			}
115		}
116		return ddxroot.Path(context.Background(), projectRoot)
117	}
118	
119	// withMainGitLock acquires the process-shared main-git lock for the
120	// given project root, runs fn, and releases the lock. The lock is a
121	// directory created via os.Mkdir (atomic across processes on POSIX and
122	// Windows) following the same pattern as cli/internal/bead/lock.go.
123	//
124	// Contention is handled per LockRetryPolicy: exponential backoff up to a
125	// bounded budget. Per TD-031 §8.5 the contention itself does not change
126	// bead status; on budget exhaustion the caller surfaces an ordinary
127	// execution_failed and triage decides next steps.
128	func withMainGitLock(projectRoot string, fn func() error) error {
129		if projectRoot == "" {
130			return fn()
131		}
132		return withTrackerLockPolicy(projectRoot, trackerLockPolicy, fn)
133	}
134	
135	// WithMainGitLock exposes the process-shared main-git lock to sibling
136	// packages that need the same serialized add/commit boundary as execute-bead
137	// landing and pre-dispatch checkpointing.
138	func WithMainGitLock(projectRoot string, fn func() error) error {
139		return withMainGitLock(projectRoot, fn)
140	}

142	// withTrackerLock is retained for existing tracker/pre-dispatch call sites. It
143	// uses the same process-shared lock as landing so separate ddx work processes
144	// cannot interleave commits, ref updates, and checkout sync in the main tree.
145	func withTrackerLock(projectRoot string, fn func() error) error {
146		return withMainGitLock(projectRoot, fn)
147	}

149	// TrackerLockSample carries timing and contention metrics for one
150	// withTrackerLock / withMainGitLock acquire+release cycle.
151	type TrackerLockSample struct {
152		LockDir string
153		Wait    time.Duration // time from entry to lock acquisition
154		Hold    time.Duration // time the callback held the lock
155		Retries int           // number of sleep-and-retry iterations (0 = acquired on first try)
156	}

158	// TrackerLockMetricsSink is called after each successful tracker-lock
159	// acquire+release cycle. The zero value (nil) is a no-op. Tests may swap
160	// this to capture metrics.

162	
163	// withTrackerLockPolicy is the policy-parameterised form of
164	// withTrackerLock; exposed at package scope so tests can pin a specific
165	// curve.
166	func withTrackerLockPolicy(projectRoot string, policy LockRetryPolicy, fn func() error) error {
167		lockDir := trackerLockPath(projectRoot)
168		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(lockDir), 0o755); err != nil {
169			return fmt.Errorf("tracker lock dir: %w", err)
170		}
171	
172		start := time.Now()
173		retries := 0
174		for attempt := 0; ; attempt++ {
175			err := os.Mkdir(lockDir, 0o755)
176			if err == nil {
177				_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(lockDir, "pid"),
178					[]byte(fmt.Sprintf("%d", os.Getpid())), 0o644)
179				_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(lockDir, "acquired_at"),
180					[]byte(time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)), 0o644)
181				break
182			}
183	
184			// Classify what exists at lockDir before deciding to sleep or fail.
185			// Per TD-031 §8.5: only a confirmed lock directory enters retry/backoff;
186			// malformed paths are operator diagnostics, not lock contention.
187			info, statErr := os.Lstat(lockDir)
188			if statErr != nil {
189				if os.IsNotExist(statErr) {
190					// Path disappeared between Mkdir and Lstat (race). Retry immediately.
191					continue
192				}
193				return fmt.Errorf("tracker lock stat: %w", statErr)
194			}
195	
196			switch {
197			case info.Mode().IsDir():
198				// Real lock directory. Apply existing stale-owner policy.
199				if breakStaleTrackerLock(lockDir) {
200					continue
201				}
202				if attempt >= policy.MaxRetries {
203					return lockTimeoutError(lockDir, "max retries")
204				}
205				if policy.MaxElapsed > 0 && time.Since(start) >= policy.MaxElapsed {
206					return lockTimeoutError(lockDir, "max elapsed")
207				}
208				retries++
209				time.Sleep(policy.step(attempt))
210	
211			case info.Mode().IsRegular():
212				// Malformed: lock path must be a directory, not a regular file.
213				if time.Since(info.ModTime()) > trackerLockStaleAge {
214					// Stale regular file: remove with single-path removal and retry immediately.
215					if rmErr := os.Remove(lockDir); rmErr != nil && !os.IsNotExist(rmErr) {
216						return fmt.Errorf("tracker lock: remove malformed stale file %s: %w", lockDir, rmErr)
217					}
218					continue
219				}
220				return fmt.Errorf("tracker lock: malformed lock path %s is a regular file; a lock directory was expected — remove it manually to recover", lockDir)
221	
222			default:
223				// Symlink, socket, device, or other special filesystem object. Do not remove.
224				return fmt.Errorf("tracker lock: malformed lock path %s has type %v; a lock directory was expected — inspect and remove it manually to recover", lockDir, info.Mode().Type())
225			}
226		}
227	
228		waitDur := time.Since(start)
229		holdStart := time.Now()
230		defer os.RemoveAll(lockDir)
231		fnErr := lockmetrics.Instrument("tracker.lock", "tracker.commit", fn)
232		if sink := TrackerLockMetricsSink; sink != nil {
233			sink(TrackerLockSample{LockDir: lockDir, Wait: waitDur, Hold: time.Since(holdStart), Retries: retries})
234		}
235		return fnErr
236	}

238	func lockTimeoutError(lockDir, why string) error {
239		owner := "missing"
240		if pidData, perr := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(lockDir, "pid")); perr == nil && len(pidData) > 0 {
241			owner = strings.TrimSpace(string(pidData))
242		}
243		return fmt.Errorf("tracker lock timeout (%s, lock: %s, owner pid: %s)", why, lockDir, owner)
244	}
245	
246	// breakStaleTrackerLock removes lockDir if its owner process is dead or the
247	// lock is older than trackerLockStaleAge. Returns true if the lock was broken.
248	func breakStaleTrackerLock(lockDir string) bool {
249		pidData, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(lockDir, "pid"))
250		if err == nil {
251			pid, err := strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSpace(string(pidData)))
252			if err == nil && pid > 0 && pid != os.Getpid() {
253				if !trackerProcessAlive(pid) {
254					os.RemoveAll(lockDir)
255					return true
256				}
257			}
258		}
259	
260		acquiredData, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(lockDir, "acquired_at"))
261		if err == nil {
262			acquired, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, strings.TrimSpace(string(acquiredData)))
263			if err == nil && time.Since(acquired) > trackerLockStaleAge {
264				os.RemoveAll(lockDir)
265				return true
266			}
267		}
268	
269		return false
270	}
